revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Nagusa Shrine | location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yabu City|ja|養父市|12=draft|12=draft}} Yōka-chō, Ishihara 1755-6 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|25|20.7|N|134|39|20.0|E|scale:20000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Nagusa Shrine}} | deity = Main deity: {{ill|Nagusahiko Ōkami|ja|名草彦大神|12=draft|12=draft}}
Secondary deities: {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主神|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇霊産神|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神皇霊産神|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Itsu-no-Takeru-no-Kami|ja|五十猛神|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōyatsuhime|ja|大屋津姫神|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kaya-no-Hime|ja|柧津姫神|12=draft|12=draft}} | type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内小社|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|12=draft|12=draft}} | founded = | festivals = {{ill|July 18|ja|7月18日|12=draft|12=draft}} | other_names = Tajima Myōken
Myōken-sha
Taisekiji Myōken-gū
Taisekiji Nikkō-in
Ishiharayama Nikkō-in | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nagusa shrine0590774.JPG|300px]]
Three-storied pagodas | map = {{location map|Japan Hyogo|lat_deg=35|lat_min=25|lat_sec=20.7|lon_deg=134|lon_min=39|lon_sec=20.0|label=Nagusa Shrine|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} [[File:Nagusa-Jinjya Hyogo001.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Nagusa Shrine''' (名草神社, Nagusa Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Yabu City|ja|養父市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|12=draft|12=draft}}. Its main deity is {{ill|Nagusahiko Ōkami|ja|名草彦大神|12=draft|12=draft}}. The shrine is situated on the slopes of {{ill|Myōken Mountain (Yabu City, Kami Town)|ja|妙見山 (養父市・香美町)|12=draft|12=draft}} (elevation 1,139m), at approximately 800m above sea level, amidst a forest of giant Myōken cedar trees. Historically, the shrine was known as "Myōken-sha" and served as a major center for Myōken (North Star) worship in the {{ill|San'in Region|ja|山陰|12=draft|12=draft}} under the patronage of feudal lords such as {{ill|Yamana Sōzen|ja|山名宗全|12=draft|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Tokugawa Shogunate|ja|徳川幕府|12=draft|12=draft}}. The shrine's main hall, worship hall, and a three-story pagoda, originally from {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社|12=draft|12=draft}}, are designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == The shrine is closely associated with {{ill|Nikkō-in|ja|日光院|12=draft|12=draft}}, a temple of the {{ill|Kōyasan Shingon School|ja|高野山真言宗|12=draft|12=draft}}, located in the Ishihara settlement at the eastern base of the mountain. Before the modern era, the shrine was known as Tajima Myōken and was regarded as one of Japan's three great Myōken sacred sites. The current name "Nagusa Shrine" was adopted following the {{ill|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja|神仏分離|12=draft|12=draft}} during the {{ill|Meiji Period|ja|明治時代|12=draft|12=draft}}. Ishiharayama Nikkō-in (later known as Taisekiji) is said to have been founded in the {{ill|Asuka Period|ja|飛鳥時代|12=draft|12=draft}}. The shrine prospered during the medieval period as a center for Myōken worship under the patronage of the Yamana clan but was devastated by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|12=draft|12=draft}}’s {{ill|Siege of Chūgoku|ja|中国攻め|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era|12=draft|12=draft}} 5 (1577). In {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永|12=draft|12=draft}} 9 (1632), the main temple of Ishiharayama was relocated to its current site, which is now Nagusa Shrine. During the Meiji separation of Shinto and Buddhism, Nikkō-in was moved to a lower temple site, while the Myōken Shrine on the mountain became Nagusa Shrine's main hall.『日本歴史地名大系 兵庫県の地名 I』（平凡社、1999）、p.777[http://japan-geographic.tv/hyogo/yabu-nagusajinja.html Nagusa Shrine - Japan Geographic] (Accessed November 2, 2013). == Grounds == === Three-story Pagoda === The pagoda was originally constructed in the {{ill|Muromachi Period|ja|室町時代|12=draft|12=draft}} during {{ill|Daiei|ja|大永|12=draft|12=draft}} 7 (1527) in the grounds of {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社|12=draft|12=draft}} by the order of {{ill|Amago Tsunehisa|ja|尼子経久|12=draft|12=draft}} and was known as the "Kizuki Tower." It was relocated to Nagusa Shrine in {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文|12=draft|12=draft}} 5 (1665) as a gift for supplying Myōken cedar timber during Izumo Taisha's reconstruction. * Built in Daiei 7 (1527), relocated in Kanbun 5 (1665), height 23.9m, three-tiered structure with cypress bark roofing * Designated a Specially Protected Building under the {{ill|Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Act|ja|古社寺保存法|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|1904|ja|明治37th year|12=draft|12=draft}}, now an Important Cultural Property under the {{ill|Cultural Properties Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法|12=draft|12=draft}} === Main Hall === * Built in {{ill|Hōreki|ja|宝暦|12=draft|12=draft}} 4 (1754). Measuring nine spans by five spans, single-story, with an irimoya roof and decorative elements * Important Cultural Property (designated in Heisei 22 with attached plaques) === Worship Hall === * Built in {{ill|Genroku|ja|元禄|12=draft|12=draft}} 2 (1689). Five spans by two spans, single-story, with a cypress bark roof * Important Cultural Property (designated in Heisei 22) == Festivals == * Head Priest Ceremony: {{ill|January 13|ja|1月13日|12=draft|12=draft}} * Spring Festival: {{ill|May 8|ja|5月8日|12=draft|12=draft}} * festival: {{ill|July 18|ja|7月18日|12=draft|12=draft}} * Autumn Festival: {{ill|October 13|ja|10月13日|12=draft|12=draft}} == Access == * Approximately 30 minutes by car from {{ill|Yōka-Hyōnoyama IC|ja|北近畿豊岡自動車道八鹿氷ノ山IC|12=draft|12=draft}}[https://www.hyogo-tourism.jp/hanadokoro/hanagoyomi.php?num=2&cat=8&area=0 Hyōgo Tourism Guide]. Inaccessible during winter due to snow.[http://www.yabu-kankou.jp/sightseeing/nagusa-shrine-haiden Yabu City Tourism Association] * Access by public transport is difficult (20 minutes by {{ill|Zentan Bus|ja|全但バス|12=draft|12=draft}} from {{ill|Yōka Station|ja|八鹿駅|12=draft|12=draft}}, followed by a 120-minute walk, approximately 9km). == Surroundings == * {{ill|Saru-otaki Falls|ja|猿尾滝|12=draft|12=draft}} - Visible from the shrine grounds * {{ill|Yabu Shrine|ja|養父神社|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Manpukuji Temple (Yabu City)|ja|満福寺|12=draft|12=draft}} - Known as "Tajima's Kōyasan" * {{ill|Nikkō-in|ja|日光院|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yamada Fūtarō Memorial Museum|ja|山田風太郎記念館|12=draft|12=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.yabu.hyogo.jp/3445.htm Nagusa Shrine - Yabu City (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://www.city.yabu.hyogo.jp/3466.htm Three-Story Pagoda from Izumo - Yabu City (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://www.city.yabu.hyogo.jp/3467.htm Nagusa Shrine on Myōken Mountain - Yabu City (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp Important Cultural Properties Database: Nagusa Shrine Pagoda (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp Important Cultural Properties Database: Nagusa Shrine Main Hall (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp Important Cultural Properties Database: Nagusa Shrine Worship Hall (Accessed November 1, 2013)] {{Shinto Navbox}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Myōken Faith}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Muromachi Period architecture]] [[Category:Edo Period architecture]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Separation of Shinto and Buddhism]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Yabu]] [[Category:History of Yabu]] [[Category:Relocated buildings in Japan]] [[Category:Nagusa Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Nagusa Shrine]] [[de:Yatsushiro Shrine]] [[ja:名草神社]] [[en:Nagusa Shrine]]

Secondary deities: {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主神|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi}}, {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇霊産神}}, {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神皇霊産神}}, {{ill|Itsu-no-Takeru-no-Kami|ja|五十猛神}}, {{ill|Ōyatsuhime|ja|大屋津姫神}}, {{ill|Kaya-no-Hime|ja|柧津姫神}} | type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内小社}}, {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} | founded = | festivals = {{ill|July 18|ja|7月18日}} | other_names = Tajima Myōken
Myōken-sha
Taisekiji Myōken-gū
Taisekiji Nikkō-in
Ishiharayama Nikkō-in | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nagusa shrine0590774.JPG|300px]]
Three-storied pagodas | map = {{location map|Japan Hyogo|lat_deg=35|lat_min=25|lat_sec=20.7|lon_deg=134|lon_min=39|lon_sec=20.0|label=Nagusa Shrine|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} [[File:Nagusa-Jinjya Hyogo001.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Nagusa Shrine''' (名草神社, Nagusa Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Yabu City|ja|養父市}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}. Its main deity is {{ill|Nagusahiko Ōkami|ja|名草彦大神}}. The shrine is situated on the slopes of {{ill|Myōken Mountain (Yabu City, Kami Town)|ja|妙見山 (養父市・香美町)}} (elevation 1,139m), at approximately 800m above sea level, amidst a forest of giant Myōken cedar trees. Historically, the shrine was known as "Myōken-sha" and served as a major center for Myōken (North Star) worship in the {{ill|San'in Region|ja|山陰}} under the patronage of feudal lords such as {{ill|Yamana Sōzen|ja|山名宗全}} and the {{ill|Tokugawa Shogunate|ja|徳川幕府}}. The shrine's main hall, worship hall, and a three-story pagoda, originally from {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社}}, are designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재}}. == History == The shrine is closely associated with {{ill|Nikkō-in|ja|日光院}}, a temple of the {{ill|Kōyasan Shingon School|ja|高野山真言宗}}, located in the Ishihara settlement at the eastern base of the mountain. Before the modern era, the shrine was known as Tajima Myōken and was regarded as one of Japan's three great Myōken sacred sites. The current name "Nagusa Shrine" was adopted following the {{ill|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja|神仏分離}} during the {{ill|Meiji Period|ja|明治時代}}. Ishiharayama Nikkō-in (later known as Taisekiji) is said to have been founded in the {{ill|Asuka Period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. The shrine prospered during the medieval period as a center for Myōken worship under the patronage of the Yamana clan but was devastated by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}’s {{ill|Siege of Chūgoku|ja|中国攻め}} in {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era}} 5 (1577). In {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永}} 9 (1632), the main temple of Ishiharayama was relocated to its current site, which is now Nagusa Shrine. During the Meiji separation of Shinto and Buddhism, Nikkō-in was moved to a lower temple site, while the Myōken Shrine on the mountain became Nagusa Shrine's main hall.『日本歴史地名大系 兵庫県の地名 I』（平凡社、1999）、p.777[http://japan-geographic.tv/hyogo/yabu-nagusajinja.html Nagusa Shrine - Japan Geographic] (Accessed November 2, 2013). == Grounds == === Three-story Pagoda === The pagoda was originally constructed in the {{ill|Muromachi Period|ja|室町時代}} during {{ill|Daiei|ja|大永}} 7 (1527) in the grounds of {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社}} by the order of {{ill|Amago Tsunehisa|ja|尼子経久}} and was known as the "Kizuki Tower." It was relocated to Nagusa Shrine in {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 5 (1665) as a gift for supplying Myōken cedar timber during Izumo Taisha's reconstruction. * Built in Daiei 7 (1527), relocated in Kanbun 5 (1665), height 23.9m, three-tiered structure with cypress bark roofing * Designated a Specially Protected Building under the {{ill|Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Act|ja|古社寺保存法}} in {{ill|1904|ja|明治37th year}}, now an Important Cultural Property under the {{ill|Cultural Properties Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法}} === Main Hall === * Built in {{ill|Hōreki|ja|宝暦}} 4 (1754). Measuring nine spans by five spans, single-story, with an irimoya roof and decorative elements * Important Cultural Property (designated in Heisei 22 with attached plaques) === Worship Hall === * Built in {{ill|Genroku|ja|元禄}} 2 (1689). Five spans by two spans, single-story, with a cypress bark roof * Important Cultural Property (designated in Heisei 22) == Festivals == * Head Priest Ceremony: {{ill|January 13|ja|1月13日}} * Spring Festival: {{ill|May 8|ja|5月8日}} * festival: {{ill|July 18|ja|7月18日}} * Autumn Festival: {{ill|October 13|ja|10月13日}} == Access == * Approximately 30 minutes by car from {{ill|Yōka-Hyōnoyama IC|ja|北近畿豊岡自動車道八鹿氷ノ山IC}}[https://www.hyogo-tourism.jp/hanadokoro/hanagoyomi.php?num=2&cat=8&area=0 Hyōgo Tourism Guide]. Inaccessible during winter due to snow.[http://www.yabu-kankou.jp/sightseeing/nagusa-shrine-haiden Yabu City Tourism Association] * Access by public transport is difficult (20 minutes by {{ill|Zentan Bus|ja|全但バス}} from {{ill|Yōka Station|ja|八鹿駅}}, followed by a 120-minute walk, approximately 9km). == Surroundings == * {{ill|Saru-otaki Falls|ja|猿尾滝}} - Visible from the shrine grounds * {{ill|Yabu Shrine|ja|養父神社}} * {{ill|Manpukuji Temple (Yabu City)|ja|満福寺}} - Known as "Tajima's Kōyasan" * {{ill|Nikkō-in|ja|日光院}} * {{ill|Yamada Fūtarō Memorial Museum|ja|山田風太郎記念館}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.yabu.hyogo.jp/3445.htm Nagusa Shrine - Yabu City (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://www.city.yabu.hyogo.jp/3466.htm Three-Story Pagoda from Izumo - Yabu City (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://www.city.yabu.hyogo.jp/3467.htm Nagusa Shrine on Myōken Mountain - Yabu City (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp Important Cultural Properties Database: Nagusa Shrine Pagoda (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp Important Cultural Properties Database: Nagusa Shrine Main Hall (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp Important Cultural Properties Database: Nagusa Shrine Worship Hall (Accessed November 1, 2013)] {{Shinto Navbox}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Myōken Faith}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Muromachi Period architecture]] [[Category:Edo Period architecture]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Separation of Shinto and Buddhism]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Yabu]] [[Category:History of Yabu]] [[Category:Relocated buildings in Japan]] [[Category:Nagusa Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Nagusa Shrine]] [[de:Yatsushiro Shrine]] [[ja:名草神社]] [[en:Nagusa Shrine]]

Yabu City (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp Important Cultural Properties Database: Nagusa Shrine Pagoda (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp Important Cultural Properties Database: Nagusa Shrine Main Hall (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp Important Cultural Properties Database: Nagusa Shrine Worship Hall (Accessed November 1, 2013)] {{Shinto Navbox}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Myōken Faith}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Muromachi Period architecture]] [[Category:Edo Period architecture]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Separation of Shinto and Buddhism]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Yabu]] [[Category:History of Yabu]] [[Category:Relocated buildings in Japan]] [[Category:Nagusa Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Nagusa Shrine]] [[de:Yatsushiro Shrine]] [[ja:名草神社]] [[en:Nagusa Shrine]] {{translated page|ja|名草神社|version=101075941|comment=Imported full ja history}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Nagusa Shrine | location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yabu City|ja|養父市|12=draft}} Yōka-chō, Ishihara 1755-6 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|25|20.7|N|134|39|20.0|E|scale:20000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Nagusa Shrine}} | deity = Main deity: {{ill|Nagusahiko Ōkami|ja|名草彦大神|12=draft}}
Secondary deities: {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主神|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi|12=draft}}, {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇霊産神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神皇霊産神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Itsu-no-Takeru-no-Kami|ja|五十猛神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōyatsuhime|ja|大屋津姫神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kaya-no-Hime|ja|柧津姫神|12=draft}} | type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内小社|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|12=draft}} | founded = | festivals = {{ill|July 18|ja|7月18日|12=draft}} | other_names = Tajima Myōken
Myōken-sha
Taisekiji Myōken-gū
Taisekiji Nikkō-in
Ishiharayama Nikkō-in | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nagusa shrine0590774.JPG|300px]]
Three-storied pagodas | map = {{location map|Japan Hyogo|lat_deg=35|lat_min=25|lat_sec=20.7|lon_deg=134|lon_min=39|lon_sec=20.0|label=Nagusa Shrine|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} [[File:Nagusa-Jinjya Hyogo001.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Nagusa Shrine''' (名草神社, Nagusa Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Yabu City|ja|養父市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|12=draft}}. Its main deity is {{ill|Nagusahiko Ōkami|ja|名草彦大神|12=draft}}. The shrine is situated on the slopes of {{ill|Myōken Mountain (Yabu City, Kami Town)|ja|妙見山 (養父市・香美町)|12=draft}} (elevation 1,139m), at approximately 800m above sea level, amidst a forest of giant Myōken cedar trees. Historically, the shrine was known as "Myōken-sha" and served as a major center for Myōken (North Star) worship in the {{ill|San'in Region|ja|山陰|12=draft}} under the patronage of feudal lords such as {{ill|Yamana Sōzen|ja|山名宗全|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Tokugawa Shogunate|ja|徳川幕府|12=draft}}. The shrine's main hall, worship hall, and a three-story pagoda, originally from {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社|12=draft}}, are designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft}}. == History == The shrine is closely associated with {{ill|Nikkō-in|ja|日光院|12=draft}}, a temple of the {{ill|Kōyasan Shingon School|ja|高野山真言宗|12=draft}}, located in the Ishihara settlement at the eastern base of the mountain. Before the modern era, the shrine was known as Tajima Myōken and was regarded as one of Japan's three great Myōken sacred sites. The current name "Nagusa Shrine" was adopted following the {{ill|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja|神仏分離|12=draft}} during the {{ill|Meiji Period|ja|明治時代|12=draft}}. Ishiharayama Nikkō-in (later known as Taisekiji) is said to have been founded in the {{ill|Asuka Period|ja|飛鳥時代|12=draft}}. The shrine prospered during the medieval period as a center for Myōken worship under the patronage of the Yamana clan but was devastated by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|12=draft}}’s {{ill|Siege of Chūgoku|ja|中国攻め|12=draft}} in {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era|12=draft}} 5 (1577). In {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永|12=draft}} 9 (1632), the main temple of Ishiharayama was relocated to its current site, which is now Nagusa Shrine. During the Meiji separation of Shinto and Buddhism, Nikkō-in was moved to a lower temple site, while the Myōken Shrine on the mountain became Nagusa Shrine's main hall.『日本歴史地名大系 兵庫県の地名 I』（平凡社、1999）、p.777[http://japan-geographic.tv/hyogo/yabu-nagusajinja.html Nagusa Shrine - Japan Geographic] (Accessed November 2, 2013). == Grounds == === Three-story Pagoda === The pagoda was originally constructed in the {{ill|Muromachi Period|ja|室町時代|12=draft}} during {{ill|Daiei|ja|大永|12=draft}} 7 (1527) in the grounds of {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社|12=draft}} by the order of {{ill|Amago Tsunehisa|ja|尼子経久|12=draft}} and was known as the "Kizuki Tower." It was relocated to Nagusa Shrine in {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文|12=draft}} 5 (1665) as a gift for supplying Myōken cedar timber during Izumo Taisha's reconstruction. * Built in Daiei 7 (1527), relocated in Kanbun 5 (1665), height 23.9m, three-tiered structure with cypress bark roofing * Designated a Specially Protected Building under the {{ill|Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Act|ja|古社寺保存法|12=draft}} in {{ill|1904|ja|明治37th year|12=draft}}, now an Important Cultural Property under the {{ill|Cultural Properties Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法|12=draft}} === Main Hall === * Built in {{ill|Hōreki|ja|宝暦|12=draft}} 4 (1754). Measuring nine spans by five spans, single-story, with an irimoya roof and decorative elements * Important Cultural Property (designated in Heisei 22 with attached plaques) === Worship Hall === * Built in {{ill|Genroku|ja|元禄|12=draft}} 2 (1689). Five spans by two spans, single-story, with a cypress bark roof * Important Cultural Property (designated in Heisei 22) == Festivals == * Head Priest Ceremony: {{ill|January 13|ja|1月13日|12=draft}} * Spring Festival: {{ill|May 8|ja|5月8日|12=draft}} * festival: {{ill|July 18|ja|7月18日|12=draft}} * Autumn Festival: {{ill|October 13|ja|10月13日|12=draft}} == Access == * Approximately 30 minutes by car from {{ill|Yōka-Hyōnoyama IC|ja|北近畿豊岡自動車道八鹿氷ノ山IC|12=draft}}[https://www.hyogo-tourism.jp/hanadokoro/hanagoyomi.php?num=2&cat=8&area=0 Hyōgo Tourism Guide]. Inaccessible during winter due to snow.[http://www.yabu-kankou.jp/sightseeing/nagusa-shrine-haiden Yabu City Tourism Association] * Access by public transport is difficult (20 minutes by {{ill|Zentan Bus|ja|全但バス|12=draft}} from {{ill|Yōka Station|ja|八鹿駅|12=draft}}, followed by a 120-minute walk, approximately 9km). == Surroundings == * {{ill|Saru-otaki Falls|ja|猿尾滝|12=draft}} - Visible from the shrine grounds * {{ill|Yabu Shrine|ja|養父神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Manpukuji Temple (Yabu City)|ja|満福寺|12=draft}} - Known as "Tajima's Kōyasan" * {{ill|Nikkō-in|ja|日光院|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yamada Fūtarō Memorial Museum|ja|山田風太郎記念館|12=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.yabu.hyogo.jp/3445.htm Nagusa Shrine - Yabu City (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://www.city.yabu.hyogo.jp/3466.htm Three-Story Pagoda from Izumo - Yabu City (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://www.city.yabu.hyogo.jp/3467.htm Nagusa Shrine on Myōken Mountain - Yabu City (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp Important Cultural Properties Database: Nagusa Shrine Pagoda (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp Important Cultural Properties Database: Nagusa Shrine Main Hall (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp Important Cultural Properties Database: Nagusa Shrine Worship Hall (Accessed November 1, 2013)] {{Shinto Navbox}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Myōken Faith}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{translated page|ja|名草神社|version=101075941|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Muromachi Period architecture]] [[Category:Edo Period architecture]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Separation of Shinto and Buddhism]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Yabu]] [[Category:History of Yabu]] [[Category:Relocated buildings in Japan]] [[Category:Nagusa Shrine]] }} [[sn:Nagusa Shrine]] [[de:Yatsushiro Shrine]] [[ja:名草神社]] [[en:Nagusa Shrine]]

During the Meiji separation of Shinto and Buddhism, Nikkō-in was moved to a lower temple site, while the Myōken Shrine on the mountain became Nagusa Shrine's main hall.『日本歴史地名大系 兵庫県の地名 I』（平凡社、1999）、p.777[http://japan-geographic.tv/hyogo/yabu-nagusajinja.html Nagusa Shrine - Japan Geographic] (Accessed November 2, 2013). == Grounds == === Three-story Pagoda === The pagoda was originally constructed in the {{ill|Muromachi Period|ja|室町時代|qq=draft}} during {{ill|Daiei|ja|大永|qq=draft}} 7 (1527) in the grounds of {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社|qq=draft}} by the order of {{ill|Amago Tsunehisa|ja|尼子経久|qq=draft}} and was known as the "Kizuki Tower." It was relocated to Nagusa Shrine in {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文|qq=draft}} 5 (1665) as a gift for supplying Myōken cedar timber during Izumo Taisha's reconstruction. * Built in Daiei 7 (1527), relocated in Kanbun 5 (1665), height 23.9m, three-tiered structure with cypress bark roofing * Designated a Specially Protected Building under the {{ill|Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Act|ja|古社寺保存法|qq=draft}} in {{ill|1904|ja|明治37th year|qq=draft}}, now an Important Cultural Property under the {{ill|Cultural Properties Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法|qq=draft}} === Main Hall === * Built in {{ill|Hōreki|ja|宝暦|qq=draft}} 4 (1754). Measuring nine spans by five spans, single-story, with an irimoya roof and decorative elements * Important Cultural Property (designated in Heisei 22 with attached plaques) === Worship Hall === * Built in {{ill|Genroku|ja|元禄|qq=draft}} 2 (1689). Five spans by two spans, single-story, with a cypress bark roof * Important Cultural Property (designated in Heisei 22) == Festivals == * Head Priest Ceremony: {{ill|January 13|ja|1月13日|qq=draft}} * Spring Festival: {{ill|May 8|ja|5月8日|qq=draft}} * festival: {{ill|July 18|ja|7月18日|qq=draft}} * Autumn Festival: {{ill|October 13|ja|10月13日|qq=draft}} == Access == * Approximately 30 minutes by car from {{ill|Yōka-Hyōnoyama IC|ja|北近畿豊岡自動車道八鹿氷ノ山IC|qq=draft}}[https://www.hyogo-tourism.jp/hanadokoro/hanagoyomi.php?num=2&cat=8&area=0 Hyōgo Tourism Guide]. Inaccessible during winter due to snow.[http://www.yabu-kankou.jp/sightseeing/nagusa-shrine-haiden Yabu City Tourism Association] * Access by public transport is difficult (20 minutes by {{ill|Zentan Bus|ja|全但バス|qq=draft}} from {{ill|Yōka Station|ja|八鹿駅|qq=draft}}, followed by a 120-minute walk, approximately 9km). == Surroundings == * {{ill|Saru-otaki Falls|ja|猿尾滝|qq=draft}} - Visible from the shrine grounds * {{ill|Yabu Shrine|ja|養父神社|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Manpukuji Temple (Yabu City)|ja|満福寺|qq=draft}} - Known as "Tajima's Kōyasan" * {{ill|Nikkō-in|ja|日光院|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Yamada Fūtarō Memorial Museum|ja|山田風太郎記念館|qq=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.yabu.hyogo.jp/3445.htm Nagusa Shrine - Yabu City (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://www.city.yabu.hyogo.jp/3466.htm Three-Story Pagoda from Izumo - Yabu City (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://www.city.yabu.hyogo.jp/3467.htm Nagusa Shrine on Myōken Mountain - Yabu City (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp Important Cultural Properties Database: Nagusa Shrine Pagoda (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp Important Cultural Properties Database: Nagusa Shrine Main Hall (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp Important Cultural Properties Database: Nagusa Shrine Worship Hall (Accessed November 1, 2013)] {{Shinto Navbox}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Myōken Faith}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{translated page|ja|名草神社|version=101075941|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Muromachi Period architecture]] [[Category:Edo Period architecture]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Separation of Shinto and Buddhism]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Yabu]] [[Category:History of Yabu]] [[Category:Relocated buildings in Japan]] [[Category:Nagusa Shrine]] }} [[sn:Nagusa Shrine]] [[de:Yatsushiro Shrine]] [[ja:名草神社]] [[en:Nagusa Shrine]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Nagusa Shrine | location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yabu City|ja|養父市|qq=draft}} Yōka-chō, Ishihara 1755-6 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|25|20.7|N|134|39|20.0|E|scale:20000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Nagusa Shrine}} | deity = Main deity: {{ill|Nagusahiko Ōkami|ja|名草彦大神|qq=draft}}
Secondary deities: {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主神|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇霊産神|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神皇霊産神|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Itsu-no-Takeru-no-Kami|ja|五十猛神|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ōyatsuhime|ja|大屋津姫神|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kaya-no-Hime|ja|柧津姫神|qq=draft}} | type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内小社|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|qq=draft}} | founded = | festivals = {{ill|July 18|ja|7月18日|qq=draft}} | other_names = Tajima Myōken
Myōken-sha
Taisekiji Myōken-gū
Taisekiji Nikkō-in
Ishiharayama Nikkō-in | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nagusa shrine0590774.JPG|300px]]
Three-storied pagodas | map = {{location map|Japan Hyogo|lat_deg=35|lat_min=25|lat_sec=20.7|lon_deg=134|lon_min=39|lon_sec=20.0|label=Nagusa Shrine|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} [[File:Nagusa-Jinjya Hyogo001.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Nagusa Shrine''' (名草神社, Nagusa Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Yabu City|ja|養父市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|qq=draft}}. Its main deity is {{ill|Nagusahiko Ōkami|ja|名草彦大神|qq=draft}}. The shrine is situated on the slopes of {{ill|Myōken Mountain (Yabu City, Kami Town)|ja|妙見山 (養父市・香美町)|qq=draft}} (elevation 1,139m), at approximately 800m above sea level, amidst a forest of giant Myōken cedar trees. Historically, the shrine was known as "Myōken-sha" and served as a major center for Myōken (North Star) worship in the {{ill|San'in Region|ja|山陰|qq=draft}} under the patronage of feudal lords such as {{ill|Yamana Sōzen|ja|山名宗全|qq=draft}} and the {{ill|Tokugawa Shogunate|ja|徳川幕府|qq=draft}}. The shrine's main hall, worship hall, and a three-story pagoda, originally from {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社|qq=draft}}, are designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft}}. == History == The shrine is closely associated with {{ill|Nikkō-in|ja|日光院|qq=draft}}, a temple of the {{ill|Kōyasan Shingon School|ja|高野山真言宗|qq=draft}}, located in the Ishihara settlement at the eastern base of the mountain. Before the modern era, the shrine was known as Tajima Myōken and was regarded as one of Japan's three great Myōken sacred sites. The current name "Nagusa Shrine" was adopted following the {{ill|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja|神仏分離|qq=draft}} during the {{ill|Meiji Period|ja|明治時代|qq=draft}}. Ishiharayama Nikkō-in (later known as Taisekiji) is said to have been founded in the {{ill|Asuka Period|ja|飛鳥時代|qq=draft}}. The shrine prospered during the medieval period as a center for Myōken worship under the patronage of the Yamana clan but was devastated by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=draft}}’s {{ill|Siege of Chūgoku|ja|中国攻め|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era|qq=draft}} 5 (1577). In {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永|qq=draft}} 9 (1632), the main temple of Ishiharayama was relocated to its current site, which is now Nagusa Shrine. During the Meiji separation of Shinto and Buddhism, Nikkō-in was moved to a lower temple site, while the Myōken Shrine on the mountain became Nagusa Shrine's main hall.『日本歴史地名大系 兵庫県の地名 I』（平凡社、1999）、p.777[http://japan-geographic.tv/hyogo/yabu-nagusajinja.html Nagusa Shrine - Japan Geographic] (Accessed November 2, 2013). == Grounds == === Three-story Pagoda === The pagoda was originally constructed in the {{ill|Muromachi Period|ja|室町時代|qq=draft}} during {{ill|Daiei|ja|大永|qq=draft}} 7 (1527) in the grounds of {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社|qq=draft}} by the order of {{ill|Amago Tsunehisa|ja|尼子経久|qq=draft}} and was known as the "Kizuki Tower." It was relocated to Nagusa Shrine in {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文|qq=draft}} 5 (1665) as a gift for supplying Myōken cedar timber during Izumo Taisha's reconstruction. * Built in Daiei 7 (1527), relocated in Kanbun 5 (1665), height 23.9m, three-tiered structure with cypress bark roofing * Designated a Specially Protected Building under the {{ill|Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Act|ja|古社寺保存法|qq=draft}} in {{ill|1904|ja|明治37th year|qq=draft}}, now an Important Cultural Property under the {{ill|Cultural Properties Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法|qq=draft}} === Main Hall === * Built in {{ill|Hōreki|ja|宝暦|qq=draft}} 4 (1754). Measuring nine spans by five spans, single-story, with an irimoya roof and decorative elements * Important Cultural Property (designated in Heisei 22 with attached plaques) === Worship Hall === * Built in {{ill|Genroku|ja|元禄|qq=draft}} 2 (1689). Five spans by two spans, single-story, with a cypress bark roof * Important Cultural Property (designated in Heisei 22) == Festivals == * Head Priest Ceremony: {{ill|January 13|ja|1月13日|qq=draft}} * Spring Festival: {{ill|May 8|ja|5月8日|qq=draft}} * festival: {{ill|July 18|ja|7月18日|qq=draft}} * Autumn Festival: {{ill|October 13|ja|10月13日|qq=draft}} == Access == * Approximately 30 minutes by car from {{ill|Yōka-Hyōnoyama IC|ja|北近畿豊岡自動車道八鹿氷ノ山IC|qq=draft}}[https://www.hyogo-tourism.jp/hanadokoro/hanagoyomi.php?num=2&cat=8&area=0 Hyōgo Tourism Guide]. Inaccessible during winter due to snow.[http://www.yabu-kankou.jp/sightseeing/nagusa-shrine-haiden Yabu City Tourism Association] * Access by public transport is difficult (20 minutes by {{ill|Zentan Bus|ja|全但バス|qq=draft}} from {{ill|Yōka Station|ja|八鹿駅|qq=draft}}, followed by a 120-minute walk, approximately 9km). == Surroundings == * {{ill|Saru-otaki Falls|ja|猿尾滝|qq=draft}} - Visible from the shrine grounds * {{ill|Yabu Shrine|ja|養父神社|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Manpukuji Temple (Yabu City)|ja|満福寺|qq=draft}} - Known as "Tajima's Kōyasan" * {{ill|Nikkō-in|ja|日光院|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Yamada Fūtarō Memorial Museum|ja|山田風太郎記念館|qq=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.yabu.hyogo.jp/3445.htm Nagusa Shrine - Yabu City (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://www.city.yabu.hyogo.jp/3466.htm Three-Story Pagoda from Izumo - Yabu City (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://www.city.yabu.hyogo.jp/3467.htm Nagusa Shrine on Myōken Mountain - Yabu City (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp Important Cultural Properties Database: Nagusa Shrine Pagoda (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp Important Cultural Properties Database: Nagusa Shrine Main Hall (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp Important Cultural Properties Database: Nagusa Shrine Worship Hall (Accessed November 1, 2013)] {{Shinto Navbox}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Myōken Faith}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{translated page|ja|名草神社|version=101075941|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Muromachi Period architecture]] [[Category:Edo Period architecture]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Separation of Shinto and Buddhism]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Yabu]] [[Category:History of Yabu]] [[Category:Relocated buildings in Japan]] [[Category:Nagusa Shrine]] }} [[sn:Nagusa Shrine]] [[de:Yatsushiro Shrine]] [[ja:名草神社]] [[en:Nagusa Shrine]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Nagusa Shrine | location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Yabu City|ja|養父市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yabu City|qq=}} Yōka-chō, Ishihara 1755-6 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|25|20.7|N|134|39|20.0|E|scale:20000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Nagusa Shrine}} | deity = Main deity: {{ill|Nagusahiko Ōkami|ja|名草彦大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagusahiko Ōkami|qq=}}
Secondary deities: {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主神|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenominakanushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|qq=}}, {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇霊産神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神皇霊産神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|qq=}}, {{ill|Itsu-no-Takeru-no-Kami|ja|五十猛神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsu-no-Takeru-no-Kami|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōyatsuhime|ja|大屋津姫神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyatsuhime|qq=}}, {{ill|Kaya-no-Hime|ja|柧津姫神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaya-no-Hime|qq=}} | type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内小社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}, {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=}} | founded = | festivals = {{ill|July 18|ja|7月18日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 18|qq=}} | other_names = Tajima Myōken
Myōken-sha
Taisekiji Myōken-gū
Taisekiji Nikkō-in
Ishiharayama Nikkō-in | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nagusa shrine0590774.JPG|300px]]
Three-storied pagodas | map = {{location map|Japan Hyogo|lat_deg=35|lat_min=25|lat_sec=20.7|lon_deg=134|lon_min=39|lon_sec=20.0|label=Nagusa Shrine|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} [[File:Nagusa-Jinjya Hyogo001.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Nagusa Shrine''' (名草神社, Nagusa Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Yabu City|ja|養父市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yabu City|qq=}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefecture|qq=}}. Its main deity is {{ill|Nagusahiko Ōkami|ja|名草彦大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagusahiko Ōkami|qq=}}. The shrine is situated on the slopes of {{ill|Myōken Mountain (Yabu City, Kami Town)|ja|妙見山 (養父市・香美町)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken Mountain (Yabu City, Kami Town)|qq=}} (elevation 1,139m), at approximately 800m above sea level, amidst a forest of giant Myōken cedar trees. Historically, the shrine was known as "Myōken-sha" and served as a major center for Myōken (North Star) worship in the {{ill|San'in Region|ja|山陰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San'in Region|qq=}} under the patronage of feudal lords such as {{ill|Yamana Sōzen|ja|山名宗全|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamana Sōzen|qq=}} and the {{ill|Tokugawa Shogunate|ja|徳川幕府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa Shogunate|qq=}}. The shrine's main hall, worship hall, and a three-story pagoda, originally from {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Taisha|qq=}}, are designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Properties of Japan|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}}. == History == The shrine is closely associated with {{ill|Nikkō-in|ja|日光院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikkō-in|qq=}}, a temple of the {{ill|Kōyasan Shingon School|ja|高野山真言宗|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōyasan Shingon School|qq=}}, located in the Ishihara settlement at the eastern base of the mountain. Before the modern era, the shrine was known as Tajima Myōken and was regarded as one of Japan's three great Myōken sacred sites. The current name "Nagusa Shrine" was adopted following the {{ill|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja|神仏分離|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|qq=}} during the {{ill|Meiji Period|ja|明治時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Period|qq=}}. Ishiharayama Nikkō-in (later known as Taisekiji) is said to have been founded in the {{ill|Asuka Period|ja|飛鳥時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Period|qq=}}. The shrine prospered during the medieval period as a center for Myōken worship under the patronage of the Yamana clan but was devastated by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyoshi|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=}}’s {{ill|Siege of Chūgoku|ja|中国攻め|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siege of Chūgoku|qq=}} in {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō (Momoyama period)|qq=}} 5 (1577). In {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan'ei|qq=}} 9 (1632), the main temple of Ishiharayama was relocated to its current site, which is now Nagusa Shrine. During the Meiji separation of Shinto and Buddhism, Nikkō-in was moved to a lower temple site, while the Myōken Shrine on the mountain became Nagusa Shrine's main hall.『日本歴史地名大系 兵庫県の地名 I』（平凡社、1999）、p.777[http://japan-geographic.tv/hyogo/yabu-nagusajinja.html Nagusa Shrine - Japan Geographic] (Accessed November 2, 2013). == Grounds == === Three-story Pagoda === The pagoda was originally constructed in the {{ill|Muromachi Period|ja|室町時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi Period|qq=}} during {{ill|Daiei|ja|大永|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daiei|qq=}} 7 (1527) in the grounds of {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Taisha|qq=}} by the order of {{ill|Amago Tsunehisa|ja|尼子経久|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amago Tsunehisa|qq=}} and was known as the "Kizuki Tower." It was relocated to Nagusa Shrine in {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbun|qq=}} 5 (1665) as a gift for supplying Myōken cedar timber during Izumo Taisha's reconstruction. * Built in Daiei 7 (1527), relocated in Kanbun 5 (1665), height 23.9m, three-tiered structure with cypress bark roofing * Designated a Specially Protected Building under the {{ill|Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Act|ja|古社寺保存法|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Act|qq=}} in {{ill|1904|ja|明治37th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1904|qq=}}, now an Important Cultural Property under the {{ill|Cultural Properties Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural Properties Protection Law|qq=}} === Main Hall === * Built in {{ill|Hōreki|ja|宝暦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōreki|qq=}} 4 (1754). Measuring nine spans by five spans, single-story, with an irimoya roof and decorative elements * Important Cultural Property (designated in Heisei 22 with attached plaques) === Worship Hall === * Built in {{ill|Genroku|ja|元禄|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genroku|qq=}} 2 (1689). Five spans by two spans, single-story, with a cypress bark roof * Important Cultural Property (designated in Heisei 22) == Festivals == * Head Priest Ceremony: {{ill|January 13|ja|1月13日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 13|qq=}} * Spring Festival: {{ill|May 8|ja|5月8日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 8|qq=}} * festival: {{ill|July 18|ja|7月18日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 18|qq=}} * Autumn Festival: {{ill|October 13|ja|10月13日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 13|qq=}} == Access == * Approximately 30 minutes by car from {{ill|Yōka-Hyōnoyama IC|ja|北近畿豊岡自動車道八鹿氷ノ山IC|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōka-Hyōnoyama IC|qq=}}[https://www.hyogo-tourism.jp/hanadokoro/hanagoyomi.php?num=2&cat=8&area=0 Hyōgo Tourism Guide]. Inaccessible during winter due to snow.[http://www.yabu-kankou.jp/sightseeing/nagusa-shrine-haiden Yabu City Tourism Association] * Access by public transport is difficult (20 minutes by {{ill|Zentan Bus|ja|全但バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zentan Bus|qq=}} from {{ill|Yōka Station|ja|八鹿駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōka Station|qq=}}, followed by a 120-minute walk, approximately 9km). == Surroundings == * {{ill|Saru-otaki Falls|ja|猿尾滝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saru-otaki Falls|qq=}} - Visible from the shrine grounds * {{ill|Yabu Shrine|ja|養父神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yabu Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Manpukuji Temple (Yabu City)|ja|満福寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manpukuji Temple (Yabu City)|qq=}} - Known as "Tajima's Kōyasan" * {{ill|Nikkō-in|ja|日光院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikkō-in|qq=}} * {{ill|Yamada Fūtarō Memorial Museum|ja|山田風太郎記念館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Fūtarō Memorial Museum|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.yabu.hyogo.jp/3445.htm Nagusa Shrine - Yabu City (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://www.city.yabu.hyogo.jp/3466.htm Three-Story Pagoda from Izumo - Yabu City (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://www.city.yabu.hyogo.jp/3467.htm Nagusa Shrine on Myōken Mountain - Yabu City (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp Important Cultural Properties Database: Nagusa Shrine Pagoda (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp Important Cultural Properties Database: Nagusa Shrine Main Hall (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp Important Cultural Properties Database: Nagusa Shrine Worship Hall (Accessed November 1, 2013)] {{Shinto Navbox}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Myōken Faith}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{translated page|ja|名草神社|version=101075941|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Muromachi Period architecture]] [[Category:Edo Period architecture]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Separation of Shinto and Buddhism]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Yabu]] [[Category:History of Yabu]] [[Category:Relocated buildings in Japan]] [[Category:Nagusa Shrine]] }} [[sn:Nagusa Shrine]] [[de:Yatsushiro Shrine]] [[ja:名草神社]] [[en:Nagusa Shrine]]

Visible from the shrine grounds * {{ill|Yabu Shrine|ja|養父神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yabu Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Manpukuji Temple (Yabu City)|ja|満福寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manpukuji Temple (Yabu City)|qq=}} - Known as "Tajima's Kōyasan" * {{ill|Nikkō-in|ja|日光院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikkō-in|qq=}} * {{ill|Yamada Fūtarō Memorial Museum|ja|山田風太郎記念館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Fūtarō Memorial Museum|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.yabu.hyogo.jp/3445.htm Nagusa Shrine - Yabu City (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://www.city.yabu.hyogo.jp/3466.htm Three-Story Pagoda from Izumo - Yabu City (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://www.city.yabu.hyogo.jp/3467.htm Nagusa Shrine on Myōken Mountain - Yabu City (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp Important Cultural Properties Database: Nagusa Shrine Pagoda (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp Important Cultural Properties Database: Nagusa Shrine Main Hall (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp Important Cultural Properties Database: Nagusa Shrine Worship Hall (Accessed November 1, 2013)] {{Shinto Navbox}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Myōken Faith}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{translated page|ja|名草神社|version=101075941|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Muromachi Period architecture]] [[Category:Edo Period architecture]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Separation of Shinto and Buddhism]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Yabu]] [[Category:History of Yabu]] [[Category:Relocated buildings in Japan]] [[Category:Nagusa Shrine]] }} [[sn:Nagusa Shrine]] [[de:Yatsushiro Shrine]] [[ja:名草神社]] [[en:Nagusa Shrine]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Nagusa Shrine | location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Yabu City|ja|養父市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yabu City|qq=}} Yōka-chō, Ishihara 1755-6 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|25|20.7|N|134|39|20.0|E|scale:20000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Nagusa Shrine}} | deity = Main deity: {{ill|Nagusahiko Ōkami|ja|名草彦大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagusahiko Ōkami|qq=}}
Secondary deities: {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主神|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenominakanushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|qq=}}, {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇霊産神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神皇霊産神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|qq=}}, {{ill|Itsu-no-Takeru-no-Kami|ja|五十猛神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsu-no-Takeru-no-Kami|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōyatsuhime|ja|大屋津姫神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyatsuhime|qq=}}, {{ill|Kaya-no-Hime|ja|柧津姫神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaya-no-Hime|qq=}} | type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内小社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}, {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=}} | founded = | festivals = {{ill|July 18|ja|7月18日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 18|qq=}} | other_names = Tajima Myōken
Myōken-sha
Taisekiji Myōken-gū
Taisekiji Nikkō-in
Ishiharayama Nikkō-in | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nagusa shrine0590774.JPG|300px]]
Three-storied pagodas | map = {{location map|Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=35|lat_min=25|lat_sec=20.7|lon_deg=134|lon_min=39|lon_sec=20.0|label=Nagusa Shrine|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} [[File:Nagusa-Jinjya Hyōgo001.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Nagusa Shrine''' (名草神社, Nagusa Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Yabu City|ja|養父市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yabu City|qq=}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefecture|qq=}}. Its main deity is {{ill|Nagusahiko Ōkami|ja|名草彦大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagusahiko Ōkami|qq=}}. The shrine is situated on the slopes of {{ill|Myōken Mountain (Yabu City, Kami Town)|ja|妙見山 (養父市・香美町)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken Mountain (Yabu City, Kami Town)|qq=}} (elevation 1,139m), at approximately 800m above sea level, amidst a forest of giant Myōken cedar trees. Historically, the shrine was known as "Myōken-sha" and served as a major center for Myōken (North Star) worship in the {{ill|San'in Region|ja|山陰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San'in Region|qq=}} under the patronage of feudal lords such as {{ill|Yamana Sōzen|ja|山名宗全|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamana Sōzen|qq=}} and the {{ill|Tokugawa Shogunate|ja|徳川幕府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa Shogunate|qq=}}. The shrine's main hall, worship hall, and a three-story pagoda, originally from {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Taisha|qq=}}, are designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Properties of Japan|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}}. == History == The shrine is closely associated with {{ill|Nikkō-in|ja|日光院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikkō-in|qq=}}, a temple of the {{ill|Kōyasan Shingon School|ja|高野山真言宗|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōyasan Shingon School|qq=}}, located in the Ishihara settlement at the eastern base of the mountain. Before the modern era, the shrine was known as Tajima Myōken and was regarded as one of Japan's three great Myōken sacred sites. The current name "Nagusa Shrine" was adopted following the {{ill|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja|神仏分離|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|qq=}} during the {{ill|Meiji Period|ja|明治|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|2=ja|3=明治|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Period|qq=}}. Ishiharayama Nikkō-in (later known as Taisekiji) is said to have been founded in the {{ill|Asuka Period|ja|飛鳥時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Period|qq=}}. The shrine prospered during the medieval period as a center for Myōken worship under the patronage of the Yamana clan but was devastated by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyoshi|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=}}’s {{ill|Siege of Chūgoku|ja|中国攻め|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siege of Chūgoku|qq=}} in {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō (Momoyama period)|qq=}} 5 (1577). In {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan'ei|qq=}} 9 (1632), the main temple of Ishiharayama was relocated to its current site, which is now Nagusa Shrine. During the Meiji separation of Shinto and Buddhism, Nikkō-in was moved to a lower temple site, while the Myōken Shrine on the mountain became Nagusa Shrine's main hall.『日本歴史地名大系 兵庫県の地名 I』（平凡社、1999）、p.777[http://japan-geographic.tv/hyogo/yabu-nagusajinja.html Nagusa Shrine - Japan Geographic] (Accessed November 2, 2013). == Grounds == === Three-story Pagoda === The pagoda was originally constructed in the {{ill|Muromachi Period|ja|室町時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi Period|qq=}} during {{ill|Daiei|ja|大永|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daiei|qq=}} 7 (1527) in the grounds of {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Taisha|qq=}} by the order of {{ill|Amago Tsunehisa|ja|尼子経久|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amago Tsunehisa|qq=}} and was known as the "Kizuki Tower." It was relocated to Nagusa Shrine in {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbun|qq=}} 5 (1665) as a gift for supplying Myōken cedar timber during Izumo Taisha's reconstruction. * Built in Daiei 7 (1527), relocated in Kanbun 5 (1665), height 23.9m, three-tiered structure with cypress bark roofing * Designated a Specially Protected Building under the {{ill|Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Act|ja|古社寺保存法|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Act|qq=}} in {{ill|1904|ja|明治37th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1904|qq=}}, now an Important Cultural Property under the {{ill|Cultural Properties Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural Properties Protection Law|qq=}} === Main Hall === * Built in {{ill|Hōreki|ja|宝暦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōreki|qq=}} 4 (1754). Measuring nine spans by five spans, single-story, with an irimoya roof and decorative elements * Important Cultural Property (designated in Heisei 22 with attached plaques) === Worship Hall === * Built in {{ill|Genroku|ja|元禄|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genroku|qq=}} 2 (1689). Five spans by two spans, single-story, with a cypress bark roof * Important Cultural Property (designated in Heisei 22) == Festivals == * Head Priest Ceremony: {{ill|January 13|ja|1月13日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 13|qq=}} * Spring Festival: {{ill|May 8|ja|5月8日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 8|qq=}} * festival: {{ill|July 18|ja|7月18日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 18|qq=}} * Autumn Festival: {{ill|October 13|ja|10月13日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 13|qq=}} == Access == * Approximately 30 minutes by car from {{ill|Yōka-Hyōnoyama IC|ja|北近畿豊岡自動車道八鹿氷ノ山IC|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōka-Hyōnoyama IC|qq=}}[https://www.hyogo-tourism.jp/hanadokoro/hanagoyomi.php?num=2&cat=8&area=0 Hyōgo Tourism Guide]. Inaccessible during winter due to snow.[http://www.yabu-kankou.jp/sightseeing/nagusa-shrine-haiden Yabu City Tourism Association] * Access by public transport is difficult (20 minutes by {{ill|Zentan Bus|ja|全但バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zentan Bus|qq=}} from {{ill|Yōka Station|ja|八鹿駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōka Station|qq=}}, followed by a 120-minute walk, approximately 9km). == Surroundings == * {{ill|Saru-otaki Falls|ja|猿尾滝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saru-otaki Falls|qq=}} - Visible from the shrine grounds * {{ill|Yabu Shrine|ja|養父神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yabu Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Manpukuji Temple (Yabu City)|ja|満福寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manpukuji Temple (Yabu City)|qq=}} - Known as "Tajima's Kōyasan" * {{ill|Nikkō-in|ja|日光院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikkō-in|qq=}} * {{ill|Yamada Fūtarō Memorial Museum|ja|山田風太郎記念館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Fūtarō Memorial Museum|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.yabu.hyogo.jp/3445.htm Nagusa Shrine - Yabu City (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://www.city.yabu.hyogo.jp/3466.htm Three-Story Pagoda from Izumo - Yabu City (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://www.city.yabu.hyogo.jp/3467.htm Nagusa Shrine on Myōken Mountain - Yabu City (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp Important Cultural Properties Database: Nagusa Shrine Pagoda (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp Important Cultural Properties Database: Nagusa Shrine Main Hall (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp Important Cultural Properties Database: Nagusa Shrine Worship Hall (Accessed November 1, 2013)] {{Shinto Navbox}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Myōken Faith}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{translated page|ja|名草神社|version=101075941|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Muromachi Period architecture]] [[Category:Edo Period architecture]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Separation of Shinto and Buddhism]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Yabu]] [[Category:History of Yabu]] [[Category:Relocated buildings in Japan]] [[Category:Nagusa Shrine]] }} [[sn:Nagusa Shrine]] [[de:Yatsushiro Shrine]] [[ja:名草神社]] [[en:Nagusa Shrine]]

It was relocated to Nagusa Shrine in {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbun|qq=}} 5 (1665) as a gift for supplying Myōken cedar timber during Izumo Taisha's reconstruction. * Built in Daiei 7 (1527), relocated in Kanbun 5 (1665), height 23.9m, three-tiered structure with cypress bark roofing * Designated a Specially Protected Building under the {{ill|Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Act|ja|古社寺保存法|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Act|qq=}} in {{ill|1904|ja|明治37th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1904|qq=}}, now an Important Cultural Property under the {{ill|Cultural Properties Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural Properties Protection Law|qq=}} === Main Hall === * Built in {{ill|Hōreki|ja|宝暦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōreki|qq=}} 4 (1754). Measuring nine spans by five spans, single-story, with an irimoya roof and decorative elements * Important Cultural Property (designated in Heisei 22 with attached plaques) === Worship Hall === * Built in {{ill|Genroku|ja|元禄|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genroku|qq=}} 2 (1689). Five spans by two spans, single-story, with a cypress bark roof * Important Cultural Property (designated in Heisei 22) == Festivals == * Head Priest Ceremony: {{ill|January 13|ja|1月13日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 13|qq=}} * Spring Festival: {{ill|May 8|ja|5月8日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 8|qq=}} * festival: {{ill|July 18|ja|7月18日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 18|qq=}} * Autumn Festival: {{ill|October 13|ja|10月13日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 13|qq=}} == Access == * Approximately 30 minutes by car from {{ill|Yōka-Hyōnoyama IC|ja|北近畿豊岡自動車道八鹿氷ノ山IC|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōka-Hyōnoyama IC|qq=}}[https://www.hyogo-tourism.jp/hanadokoro/hanagoyomi.php?num=2&cat=8&area=0 Hyōgo Tourism Guide]. Inaccessible during winter due to snow.[http://www.yabu-kankou.jp/sightseeing/nagusa-shrine-haiden Yabu City Tourism Association] * Access by public transport is difficult (20 minutes by {{ill|Zentan Bus|ja|全但バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zentan Bus|qq=}} from {{ill|Yōka Station|ja|八鹿駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōka Station|qq=}}, followed by a 120-minute walk, approximately 9km). == Surroundings == * {{ill|Saru-otaki Falls|ja|猿尾滝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saru-otaki Falls|qq=}} - Visible from the shrine grounds * {{ill|Yabu Shrine|ja|養父神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yabu Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Manpukuji Temple (Yabu City)|ja|満福寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manpukuji Temple (Yabu City)|qq=}} - Known as "Tajima's Kōyasan" * {{ill|Nikkō-in|ja|日光院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikkō-in|qq=}} * {{ill|Yamada Fūtarō Memorial Museum|ja|山田風太郎記念館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Fūtarō Memorial Museum|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.yabu.hyogo.jp/3445.htm Nagusa Shrine - Yabu City (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp Important Cultural Properties Database: Nagusa Shrine Pagoda (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp Important Cultural Properties Database: Nagusa Shrine Main Hall (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp Important Cultural Properties Database: Nagusa Shrine Worship Hall (Accessed November 1, 2013)] {{Shinto Navbox}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Myōken Faith}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{translated page|ja|名草神社|version=101075941|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Muromachi Period architecture]] [[Category:Edo Period architecture]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Separation of Shinto and Buddhism]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Yabu]] [[Category:History of Yabu]] [[Category:Relocated buildings in Japan]] [[Category:Nagusa Shrine]] }} [[sn:Nagusa Shrine]] [[de:Yatsushiro Shrine]] [[ja:名草神社]] [[en:Nagusa Shrine]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Nagusa Shrine | location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Yabu City|ja|養父市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yabu City|qq=}} Yōka-chō, Ishihara 1755-6 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|25|20.7|N|134|39|20.0|E|scale:20000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Nagusa Shrine}} | deity = Main deity: {{ill|Nagusahiko Ōkami|ja|名草彦大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagusahiko Ōkami|qq=}}
Secondary deities: {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主神|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenominakanushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|qq=}}, {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇霊産神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神皇霊産神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|qq=}}, {{ill|Itsu-no-Takeru-no-Kami|ja|五十猛神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsu-no-Takeru-no-Kami|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōyatsuhime|ja|大屋津姫神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyatsuhime|qq=}}, {{ill|Kaya-no-Hime|ja|柧津姫神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaya-no-Hime|qq=}} | type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内小社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}, {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=}} | founded = | festivals = {{ill|July 18|ja|7月18日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 18|qq=}} | other_names = Tajima Myōken
Myōken-sha
Taisekiji Myōken-gū
Taisekiji Nikkō-in
Ishiharayama Nikkō-in | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nagusa shrine0590774.JPG|300px]]
Three-storied pagodas | map = {{location map|Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=35|lat_min=25|lat_sec=20.7|lon_deg=134|lon_min=39|lon_sec=20.0|label=Nagusa Shrine|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} [[File:Nagusa-Jinjya Hyōgo001.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Nagusa Shrine''' (名草神社, Nagusa Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Yabu City|ja|養父市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yabu City|qq=}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefecture|qq=}}. Its main deity is {{ill|Nagusahiko Ōkami|ja|名草彦大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagusahiko Ōkami|qq=}}. The shrine is situated on the slopes of {{ill|Myōken Mountain (Yabu City, Kami Town)|ja|妙見山 (養父市・香美町)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken Mountain (Yabu City, Kami Town)|qq=}} (elevation 1,139m), at approximately 800m above sea level, amidst a forest of giant Myōken cedar trees. Historically, the shrine was known as "Myōken-sha" and served as a major center for Myōken (North Star) worship in the {{ill|San'in Region|ja|山陰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San'in Region|qq=}} under the patronage of feudal lords such as {{ill|Yamana Sōzen|ja|山名宗全|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamana Sōzen|qq=}} and the {{ill|Tokugawa Shogunate|ja|徳川幕府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa Shogunate|qq=}}. The shrine's main hall, worship hall, and a three-story pagoda, originally from {{ill|Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Taisha|qq=}}, are designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties of Japan|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Properties of Japan|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}}. == History == The shrine is closely associated with {{ill|Nikkō-in|ja|日光院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikkō-in|qq=}}, a temple of the {{ill|Kōyasan Shingon School|ja|高野山真言宗|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōyasan Shingon School|qq=}}, located in the Ishihara settlement at the eastern base of the mountain. Before the modern era, the shrine was known as Tajima Myōken and was regarded as one of Japan's three great Myōken sacred sites. The current name "Nagusa Shrine" was adopted following the {{ill|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja|神仏分離|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|qq=}} during the {{ill|Meiji Period|ja|明治|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|2=ja|3=明治|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Period|qq=}}. Ishiharayama Nikkō-in (later known as Taisekiji) is said to have been founded in the {{ill|Asuka Period|ja|飛鳥時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Period|qq=}}. The shrine prospered during the medieval period as a center for Myōken worship under the patronage of the Yamana clan but was devastated by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyoshi|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=}}’s {{ill|Siege of Chūgoku|ja|中国攻め|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siege of Chūgoku|qq=}} in {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō (Momoyama period)|qq=}} 5 (1577). In {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan'ei|qq=}} 9 (1632), the main temple of Ishiharayama was relocated to its current site, which is now Nagusa Shrine. During the Meiji separation of Shinto and Buddhism, Nikkō-in was moved to a lower temple site, while the Myōken Shrine on the mountain became Nagusa Shrine's main hall.『日本歴史地名大系 兵庫県の地名 I』（平凡社、1999）、p.777[http://japan-geographic.tv/hyogo/yabu-nagusajinja.html Nagusa Shrine - Japan Geographic] (Accessed November 2, 2013). == Grounds == === Three-story Pagoda === The pagoda was originally constructed in the {{ill|Muromachi Period|ja|室町時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi Period|qq=}} during {{ill|Daiei|ja|大永|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daiei|qq=}} 7 (1527) in the grounds of {{ill|Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Taisha|qq=}} by the order of {{ill|Amago Tsunehisa|ja|尼子経久|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amago Tsunehisa|qq=}} and was known as the "Kizuki Tower." It was relocated to Nagusa Shrine in {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbun|qq=}} 5 (1665) as a gift for supplying Myōken cedar timber during Izumo Taisha's reconstruction. * Built in Daiei 7 (1527), relocated in Kanbun 5 (1665), height 23.9m, three-tiered structure with cypress bark roofing * Designated a Specially Protected Building under the {{ill|Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Act|ja|古社寺保存法|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Act|qq=}} in {{ill|1904|ja|明治37th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1904|qq=}}, now an Important Cultural Property under the {{ill|Cultural Properties Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural Properties Protection Law|qq=}} === Main Hall === * Built in {{ill|Hōreki|ja|宝暦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōreki|qq=}} 4 (1754). Measuring nine spans by five spans, single-story, with an irimoya roof and decorative elements * Important Cultural Property (designated in Heisei 22 with attached plaques) === Worship Hall === * Built in {{ill|Genroku|ja|元禄|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genroku|qq=}} 2 (1689). Five spans by two spans, single-story, with a cypress bark roof * Important Cultural Property (designated in Heisei 22) == Festivals == * Head Priest Ceremony: {{ill|January 13|ja|1月13日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 13|qq=}} * Spring Festival: {{ill|May 8|ja|5月8日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 8|qq=}} * festival: {{ill|July 18|ja|7月18日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 18|qq=}} * Autumn Festival: {{ill|October 13|ja|10月13日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 13|qq=}} == Access == * Approximately 30 minutes by car from {{ill|Yōka-Hyōnoyama IC|ja|北近畿豊岡自動車道八鹿氷ノ山IC|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōka-Hyōnoyama IC|qq=}}[https://www.hyogo-tourism.jp/hanadokoro/hanagoyomi.php?num=2&cat=8&area=0 Hyōgo Tourism Guide]. Inaccessible during winter due to snow.[http://www.yabu-kankou.jp/sightseeing/nagusa-shrine-haiden Yabu City Tourism Association] * Access by public transport is difficult (20 minutes by {{ill|Zentan Bus|ja|全但バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zentan Bus|qq=}} from {{ill|Yōka Station|ja|八鹿駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōka Station|qq=}}, followed by a 120-minute walk, approximately 9km). == Surroundings == * {{ill|Saru-otaki Falls|ja|猿尾滝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saru-otaki Falls|qq=}} - Visible from the shrine grounds * {{ill|Yabu Shrine|ja|養父神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yabu Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Manpukuji Temple (Yabu City)|ja|満福寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manpukuji Temple (Yabu City)|qq=}} - Known as "Tajima's Kōyasan" * {{ill|Nikkō-in|ja|日光院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikkō-in|qq=}} * {{ill|Yamada Fūtarō Memorial Museum|ja|山田風太郎記念館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Fūtarō Memorial Museum|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.yabu.hyogo.jp/3445.htm Nagusa Shrine - Yabu City (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://www.city.yabu.hyogo.jp/3466.htm Three-Story Pagoda from Izumo - Yabu City (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://www.city.yabu.hyogo.jp/3467.htm Nagusa Shrine on Myōken Mountain - Yabu City (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp Important Cultural Properties Database: Nagusa Shrine Pagoda (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp Important Cultural Properties Database: Nagusa Shrine Main Hall (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp Important Cultural Properties Database: Nagusa Shrine Worship Hall (Accessed November 1, 2013)] {{Shinto Navbox}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Myōken Faith}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{translated page|ja|名草神社|version=101075941|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Edo Period architecture]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Separation of Shinto and Buddhism]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Yabu]] [[Category:History of Yabu]] [[Category:Relocated buildings in Japan]] [[Category:Nagusa Shrine]] }} [[sn:Nagusa Shrine]] [[de:Yatsushiro Shrine]] [[ja:名草神社]] [[en:Nagusa Shrine]]
