The shrine prospered during the medieval period as a center for Myōken worship under the patronage of the Yamana clan but was devastated by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]’s {{ill|Siege of Chūgoku|ja|中国攻め|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siege of Chūgoku|qq=}} in [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 5 (1577). In [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 9 (1632), the main temple of Ishiharayama was relocated to its current site, which is now Nagusa Shrine. During the Meiji separation of Shinto and Buddhism, Nikkō-in was moved to a lower temple site, while the Myōken Shrine on the mountain became Nagusa Shrine's main hall.『日本歴史地名大系 兵庫県の地名 I』（平凡社、1999）、p.777[http://japan-geographic.tv/hyogo/yabu-nagusajinja.html Nagusa Shrine - Japan Geographic] (Accessed November 2, 2013). == Grounds == === Three-story Pagoda === The pagoda was originally constructed in the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi Period]] during [[:en:Daiei (era)|Daiei]] 7 (1527) in the grounds of [[:en:Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]] by the order of [[:en:Amago Tsunehisa|Amago Tsunehisa]] and was known as the "Kizuki Tower." It was relocated to Nagusa Shrine in [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 5 (1665) as a gift for supplying Myōken cedar timber during Izumo Taisha's reconstruction. * Built in Daiei 7 (1527), relocated in Kanbun 5 (1665), height 23.9m, three-tiered structure with cypress bark roofing * Designated a Specially Protected Building under the {{ill|Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Act|ja|古社寺保存法|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Act|qq=}} in [[:en:1904|Meiji 37]][[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]], now an Important Cultural Property under the {{ill|Cultural Properties Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural Properties Protection Law|qq=}} === Main Hall === * Built in [[:en:Hōreki|Hōreki]] 4 (1754). Measuring nine spans by five spans, single-story, with an irimoya roof and decorative elements * Important Cultural Property (designated in Heisei 22 with attached plaques) === Worship Hall === * Built in [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 2 (1689). Five spans by two spans, single-story, with a cypress bark roof * Important Cultural Property (designated in Heisei 22) == Festivals == * Head Priest Ceremony: [[:en:January 13|January 13]] * Spring Festival: [[:en:May 8|May 8]] * festival: [[:en:July 18|July 18]] * Autumn Festival: [[:en:October 13|October 13]] == Access == * Approximately 30 minutes by car from {{ill|Yōka-Hyōnoyama IC|ja|北近畿豊岡自動車道八鹿氷ノ山IC|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōka-Hyōnoyama IC|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}[https://www.hyogo-tourism.jp/hanadokoro/hanagoyomi.php?num=2&cat=8&area=0 Hyōgo Tourism Guide]. Inaccessible during winter due to snow.[http://www.yabu-kankou.jp/sightseeing/nagusa-shrine-haiden Yabu City Tourism Association] * Access by public transport is difficult (20 minutes by {{ill|Zentan Bus|ja|全但バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zentan Bus|qq=}} from [[:en:Yōka Station|Yōka Station]], followed by a 120-minute walk, approximately 9km). == Surroundings == * {{ill|Saru-otaki Falls|ja|猿尾滝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saru-otaki Falls|qq=}} - Visible from the shrine grounds * {{ill|Yabu Shrine|ja|養父神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yabu Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Manpukuji Temple (Yabu City)|ja|満福寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manpukuji Temple (Yabu City)|qq=}} - Known as "Tajima's Kōyasan" * {{ill|Nikkō-in|ja|日光院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikkō-in|qq=}} * {{ill|Yamada Fūtarō Memorial Museum|ja|山田風太郎記念館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Fūtarō Memorial Museum|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.yabu.hyogo.jp/3445.htm Nagusa Shrine - Yabu City (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp Important Cultural Properties Database: Nagusa Shrine Pagoda (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp Important Cultural Properties Database: Nagusa Shrine Main Hall (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp Important Cultural Properties Database: Nagusa Shrine Worship Hall (Accessed November 1, 2013)] {{Shinto Navbox}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Myōken Faith}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{translated page|ja|名草神社|version=101075941|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Separation of Shinto and Buddhism]] [[Category:History of Yabu]] [[Category:Relocated buildings in Japan]] [[Category:run it on these again]][[sn:Nagusa Shrine]][Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nagusa Shrine]] [[de:Yatsushiro Shrine]] [[ja:名草神社]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Shinbutsu bunri]] [[Category:Relocated buildings and structures]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Buildings in Yabu, Hyogo]] [[Category:History of Yabu, Hyogo]] {{Commons category|Nagusa-jinja}}

Secondary deities: {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主神|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenominakanushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}}, {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇霊産神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神皇霊産神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Itsu-no-Takeru-no-Kami|ja|五十猛神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsu-no-Takeru-no-Kami|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōyatsuhime|ja|大屋津姫神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyatsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kaya-no-Hime|ja|柧津姫神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaya-no-Hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内小社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}, {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} | founded = | festivals = [[:en:July 18|July 18]] | other_names = Tajima Myōken
Myōken-sha
Taisekiji Myōken-gū
Taisekiji Nikkō-in
Ishiharayama Nikkō-in | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nagusa shrine0590774.JPG|300px]]
Three-storied pagodas | map = {{location map|Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=35|lat_min=25|lat_sec=20.7|lon_deg=134|lon_min=39|lon_sec=20.0|label=Nagusa Shrine|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} [[File:Nagusa-Jinjya Hyōgo001.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Nagusa Shrine''' (名草神社, Nagusa Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Yabu, Hyōgo|Yabu City]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. Its main deity is {{ill|Nagusahiko Ōkami|ja|名草彦大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagusahiko Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The shrine is situated on the slopes of [[:en:Mount Myōken (Tajima)|Myōken Mountain (Yabu City, Kami Town)]] (elevation 1,139m), at approximately 800m above sea level, amidst a forest of giant Myōken cedar trees. Historically, the shrine was known as "Myōken-sha" and served as a major center for Myōken (North Star) worship in the {{ill|San'in Region|ja|山陰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San'in Region|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山陰地方|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山陰地方}} under the patronage of feudal lords such as [[:en:Yamana Sōzen|Yamana Sōzen]] and the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|lt=Tokugawa shogunate|lt=Tokugawa Shogunate|ja|徳川幕府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa Shogunate|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}}. The shrine's main hall, worship hall, and a three-story pagoda, originally from [[:en:Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]], are designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. == History == The shrine is closely associated with {{ill|Nikkō-in|ja|日光院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikkō-in|qq=}}, a temple of the [[:en:Kōyasan Shingon-shū|Kōyasan Shingon School]], located in the Ishihara settlement at the eastern base of the mountain. Before the modern era, the shrine was known as Tajima Myōken and was regarded as one of Japan's three great Myōken sacred sites. The current name "Nagusa Shrine" was adopted following the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism]] during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]]. Ishiharayama Nikkō-in (later known as Taisekiji) is said to have been founded in the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka Period]]. The shrine prospered during the medieval period as a center for Myōken worship under the patronage of the Yamana clan but was devastated by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]’s {{ill|Siege of Chūgoku|ja|中国攻め|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siege of Chūgoku|qq=}} in [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 5 (1577). In [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 9 (1632), the main temple of Ishiharayama was relocated to its current site, which is now Nagusa Shrine. During the Meiji separation of Shinto and Buddhism, Nikkō-in was moved to a lower temple site, while the Myōken Shrine on the mountain became Nagusa Shrine's main hall.『日本歴史地名大系 兵庫県の地名 I』（平凡社、1999）、p.777[http://japan-geographic.tv/hyogo/yabu-nagusajinja.html Nagusa Shrine - Japan Geographic] (Accessed November 2, 2013). == Grounds == === Three-story Pagoda === The pagoda was originally constructed in the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi Period]] during [[:en:Daiei (era)|Daiei]] 7 (1527) in the grounds of [[:en:Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]] by the order of [[:en:Amago Tsunehisa|Amago Tsunehisa]] and was known as the "Kizuki Tower." It was relocated to Nagusa Shrine in [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 5 (1665) as a gift for supplying Myōken cedar timber during Izumo Taisha's reconstruction. * Built in Daiei 7 (1527), relocated in Kanbun 5 (1665), height 23.9m, three-tiered structure with cypress bark roofing * Designated a Specially Protected Building under the {{ill|Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Act|ja|古社寺保存法|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Act|qq=}} in [[:en:1904|Meiji 37]][[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]], now an Important Cultural Property under the {{ill|Cultural Properties Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural Properties Protection Law|qq=}} === Main Hall === * Built in [[:en:Hōreki|Hōreki]] 4 (1754). Measuring nine spans by five spans, single-story, with an irimoya roof and decorative elements * Important Cultural Property (designated in Heisei 22 with attached plaques) === Worship Hall === * Built in [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 2 (1689). Five spans by two spans, single-story, with a cypress bark roof * Important Cultural Property (designated in Heisei 22) == Festivals == * Head Priest Ceremony: [[:en:January 13|January 13]] * Spring Festival: [[:en:May 8|May 8]] * festival: [[:en:July 18|July 18]] * Autumn Festival: [[:en:October 13|October 13]] == Access == * Approximately 30 minutes by car from {{ill|Yōka-Hyōnoyama IC|ja|北近畿豊岡自動車道八鹿氷ノ山IC|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōka-Hyōnoyama IC|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}[https://www.hyogo-tourism.jp/hanadokoro/hanagoyomi.php?num=2&cat=8&area=0 Hyōgo Tourism Guide]. Inaccessible during winter due to snow.[http://www.yabu-kankou.jp/sightseeing/nagusa-shrine-haiden Yabu City Tourism Association] * Access by public transport is difficult (20 minutes by {{ill|Zentan Bus|ja|全但バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zentan Bus|qq=}} from [[:en:Yōka Station|Yōka Station]], followed by a 120-minute walk, approximately 9km). == Surroundings == * {{ill|Saru-otaki Falls|ja|猿尾滝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saru-otaki Falls|qq=}} - Visible from the shrine grounds * {{ill|Yabu Shrine|ja|養父神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yabu Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Manpukuji Temple (Yabu City)|ja|満福寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manpukuji Temple (Yabu City)|qq=}} - Known as "Tajima's Kōyasan" * {{ill|Nikkō-in|ja|日光院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikkō-in|qq=}} * {{ill|Yamada Fūtarō Memorial Museum|ja|山田風太郎記念館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Fūtarō Memorial Museum|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.yabu.hyogo.jp/3445.htm Nagusa Shrine - Yabu City (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp Important Cultural Properties Database: Nagusa Shrine Pagoda (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp Important Cultural Properties Database: Nagusa Shrine Main Hall (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp Important Cultural Properties Database: Nagusa Shrine Worship Hall (Accessed November 1, 2013)] {{Shinto Navbox}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Myōken Faith}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{translated page|ja|名草神社|version=101075941|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Separation of Shinto and Buddhism]] [[Category:Relocated buildings in Japan]] [[Category:run it on these again]][[sn:Nagusa Shrine]][Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nagusa Shrine]] [[de:Yatsushiro Shrine]] [[ja:名草神社]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Shinbutsu bunri]] [[Category:Relocated buildings and structures]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Buildings in Yabu, Hyogo]] {{Commons category|Nagusa-jinja}}

It was relocated to Nagusa Shrine in [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 5 (1665) as a gift for supplying Myōken cedar timber during Izumo Taisha's reconstruction. * Built in Daiei 7 (1527), relocated in Kanbun 5 (1665), height 23.9m, three-tiered structure with cypress bark roofing * Designated a Specially Protected Building under the {{ill|Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Act|ja|古社寺保存法|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Act|qq=}} in [[:en:1904|Meiji 37]][[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]], now an Important Cultural Property under the {{ill|Cultural Properties Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural Properties Protection Law|qq=}} === Main Hall === * Built in [[:en:Hōreki|Hōreki]] 4 (1754). Measuring nine spans by five spans, single-story, with an irimoya roof and decorative elements * Important Cultural Property (designated in Heisei 22 with attached plaques) === Worship Hall === * Built in [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 2 (1689). Five spans by two spans, single-story, with a cypress bark roof * Important Cultural Property (designated in Heisei 22) == Festivals == * Head Priest Ceremony: [[:en:January 13|January 13]] * Spring Festival: [[:en:May 8|May 8]] * festival: [[:en:July 18|July 18]] * Autumn Festival: [[:en:October 13|October 13]] == Access == * Approximately 30 minutes by car from {{ill|Yōka-Hyōnoyama IC|ja|北近畿豊岡自動車道八鹿氷ノ山IC|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōka-Hyōnoyama IC|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}[https://www.hyogo-tourism.jp/hanadokoro/hanagoyomi.php?num=2&cat=8&area=0 Hyōgo Tourism Guide]. Inaccessible during winter due to snow.[http://www.yabu-kankou.jp/sightseeing/nagusa-shrine-haiden Yabu City Tourism Association] * Access by public transport is difficult (20 minutes by {{ill|Zentan Bus|ja|全但バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zentan Bus|qq=}} from [[:en:Yōka Station|Yōka Station]], followed by a 120-minute walk, approximately 9km). == Surroundings == * {{ill|Saru-otaki Falls|ja|猿尾滝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saru-otaki Falls|qq=}} - Visible from the shrine grounds * {{ill|Yabu Shrine|ja|養父神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yabu Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Manpukuji Temple (Yabu City)|ja|満福寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manpukuji Temple (Yabu City)|qq=}} - Known as "Tajima's Kōyasan" * {{ill|Nikkō-in|ja|日光院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikkō-in|qq=}} * {{ill|Yamada Fūtarō Memorial Museum|ja|山田風太郎記念館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Fūtarō Memorial Museum|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.yabu.hyogo.jp/3445.htm Nagusa Shrine - Yabu City (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://www.city.yabu.hyogo.jp/3466.htm Three-Story Pagoda from Izumo - Yabu City (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://www.city.yabu.hyogo.jp/3467.htm Nagusa Shrine on Myōken Mountain - Yabu City (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp Important Cultural Properties Database: Nagusa Shrine Pagoda (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp Important Cultural Properties Database: Nagusa Shrine Main Hall (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp Important Cultural Properties Database: Nagusa Shrine Worship Hall (Accessed November 1, 2013)] {{Shinto Navbox}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Myōken Faith}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{translated page|ja|名草神社|version=101075941|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Separation of Shinto and Buddhism]] [[Category:run it on these again]][[sn:Nagusa Shrine]][Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nagusa Shrine]] [[de:Yatsushiro Shrine]] [[ja:名草神社]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Shinbutsu bunri]] [[Category:Relocated buildings and structures]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Buildings in Yabu, Hyogo]] {{Commons category|Nagusa-jinja}}

Secondary deities: {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主神|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenominakanushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}}, {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇霊産神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神皇霊産神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Itsu-no-Takeru-no-Kami|ja|五十猛神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsu-no-Takeru-no-Kami|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōyatsuhime|ja|大屋津姫神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyatsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kaya-no-Hime|ja|柧津姫神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaya-no-Hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内小社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}, {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} | founded = | festivals = [[:en:July 18|July 18]] | other_names = Tajima Myōken
Myōken-sha
Taisekiji Myōken-gū
Taisekiji Nikkō-in
Ishiharayama Nikkō-in | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nagusa shrine0590774.JPG|300px]]
Three-storied pagodas | map = {{location map|Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=35|lat_min=25|lat_sec=20.7|lon_deg=134|lon_min=39|lon_sec=20.0|label=Nagusa Shrine|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} [[File:Nagusa-Jinjya Hyōgo001.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Nagusa Shrine''' (名草神社, Nagusa Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Yabu, Hyōgo|Yabu City]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. Its main deity is {{ill|Nagusahiko Ōkami|ja|名草彦大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagusahiko Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The shrine is situated on the slopes of [[:en:Mount Myōken (Tajima)|Myōken Mountain (Yabu City, Kami Town)]] (elevation 1,139m), at approximately 800m above sea level, amidst a forest of giant Myōken cedar trees. Historically, the shrine was known as "Myōken-sha" and served as a major center for Myōken (North Star) worship in the {{ill|San'in Region|ja|山陰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San'in Region|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山陰地方|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山陰地方}} under the patronage of feudal lords such as [[:en:Yamana Sōzen|Yamana Sōzen]] and the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|lt=Tokugawa shogunate|lt=Tokugawa Shogunate|ja|徳川幕府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa Shogunate|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}}. The shrine's main hall, worship hall, and a three-story pagoda, originally from [[:en:Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]], are designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. == History == The shrine is closely associated with {{ill|Nikkō-in|ja|日光院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikkō-in|qq=}}, a temple of the [[:en:Kōyasan Shingon-shū|Kōyasan Shingon School]], located in the Ishihara settlement at the eastern base of the mountain. Before the modern era, the shrine was known as Tajima Myōken and was regarded as one of Japan's three great Myōken sacred sites. The current name "Nagusa Shrine" was adopted following the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism]] during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]]. Ishiharayama Nikkō-in (later known as Taisekiji) is said to have been founded in the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka Period]]. The shrine prospered during the medieval period as a center for Myōken worship under the patronage of the Yamana clan but was devastated by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]’s {{ill|Siege of Chūgoku|ja|中国攻め|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siege of Chūgoku|qq=}} in [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 5 (1577). In [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 9 (1632), the main temple of Ishiharayama was relocated to its current site, which is now Nagusa Shrine. During the Meiji separation of Shinto and Buddhism, Nikkō-in was moved to a lower temple site, while the Myōken Shrine on the mountain became Nagusa Shrine's main hall.『日本歴史地名大系 兵庫県の地名 I』（平凡社、1999）、p.777[http://japan-geographic.tv/hyogo/yabu-nagusajinja.html Nagusa Shrine - Japan Geographic] (Accessed November 2, 2013). == Grounds == === Three-story Pagoda === The pagoda was originally constructed in the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi Period]] during [[:en:Daiei (era)|Daiei]] 7 (1527) in the grounds of [[:en:Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]] by the order of [[:en:Amago Tsunehisa|Amago Tsunehisa]] and was known as the "Kizuki Tower." It was relocated to Nagusa Shrine in [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 5 (1665) as a gift for supplying Myōken cedar timber during Izumo Taisha's reconstruction. * Built in Daiei 7 (1527), relocated in Kanbun 5 (1665), height 23.9m, three-tiered structure with cypress bark roofing * Designated a Specially Protected Building under the {{ill|Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Act|ja|古社寺保存法|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Act|qq=}} in [[:en:1904|Meiji 37]][[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]], now an Important Cultural Property under the {{ill|Cultural Properties Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural Properties Protection Law|qq=}} === Main Hall === * Built in [[:en:Hōreki|Hōreki]] 4 (1754). Measuring nine spans by five spans, single-story, with an irimoya roof and decorative elements * Important Cultural Property (designated in Heisei 22 with attached plaques) === Worship Hall === * Built in [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 2 (1689). Five spans by two spans, single-story, with a cypress bark roof * Important Cultural Property (designated in Heisei 22) == Festivals == * Head Priest Ceremony: [[:en:January 13|January 13]] * Spring Festival: [[:en:May 8|May 8]] * festival: [[:en:July 18|July 18]] * Autumn Festival: [[:en:October 13|October 13]] == Access == * Approximately 30 minutes by car from {{ill|Yōka-Hyōnoyama IC|ja|北近畿豊岡自動車道八鹿氷ノ山IC|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōka-Hyōnoyama IC|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}[https://www.hyogo-tourism.jp/hanadokoro/hanagoyomi.php?num=2&cat=8&area=0 Hyōgo Tourism Guide]. Inaccessible during winter due to snow.[http://www.yabu-kankou.jp/sightseeing/nagusa-shrine-haiden Yabu City Tourism Association] * Access by public transport is difficult (20 minutes by {{ill|Zentan Bus|ja|全但バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zentan Bus|qq=}} from [[:en:Yōka Station|Yōka Station]], followed by a 120-minute walk, approximately 9km). == Surroundings == * {{ill|Saru-otaki Falls|ja|猿尾滝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saru-otaki Falls|qq=}} - Visible from the shrine grounds * {{ill|Yabu Shrine|ja|養父神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yabu Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Manpukuji Temple (Yabu City)|ja|満福寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manpukuji Temple (Yabu City)|qq=}} - Known as "Tajima's Kōyasan" * {{ill|Nikkō-in|ja|日光院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikkō-in|qq=}} * {{ill|Yamada Fūtarō Memorial Museum|ja|山田風太郎記念館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Fūtarō Memorial Museum|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.yabu.hyogo.jp/3445.htm Nagusa Shrine - Yabu City (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://www.city.yabu.hyogo.jp/3466.htm Three-Story Pagoda from Izumo - Yabu City (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://www.city.yabu.hyogo.jp/3467.htm Nagusa Shrine on Myōken Mountain - Yabu City (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp Important Cultural Properties Database: Nagusa Shrine Pagoda (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp Important Cultural Properties Database: Nagusa Shrine Main Hall (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp Important Cultural Properties Database: Nagusa Shrine Worship Hall (Accessed November 1, 2013)] {{Shinto Navbox}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Myōken Faith}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{translated page|ja|名草神社|version=101075941|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Separation of Shinto and Buddhism]] [[sn:Nagusa Shrine]][Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nagusa Shrine]] [[de:Yatsushiro Shrine]] [[ja:名草神社]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Shinbutsu bunri]] [[Category:Relocated buildings and structures]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Buildings in Yabu, Hyogo]] {{Commons category|Nagusa-jinja}}

Measuring nine spans by five spans, single-story, with an irimoya roof and decorative elements * Important Cultural Property (designated in Heisei 22 with attached plaques) === Worship Hall === * Built in [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 2 (1689). Five spans by two spans, single-story, with a cypress bark roof * Important Cultural Property (designated in Heisei 22) == Festivals == * Head Priest Ceremony: [[:en:January 13|January 13]] * Spring Festival: [[:en:May 8|May 8]] * festival: [[:en:July 18|July 18]] * Autumn Festival: [[:en:October 13|October 13]] == Access == * Approximately 30 minutes by car from {{ill|Yōka-Hyōnoyama IC|ja|北近畿豊岡自動車道八鹿氷ノ山IC|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōka-Hyōnoyama IC|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}[https://www.hyogo-tourism.jp/hanadokoro/hanagoyomi.php?num=2&cat=8&area=0 Hyōgo Tourism Guide]. Inaccessible during winter due to snow.[http://www.yabu-kankou.jp/sightseeing/nagusa-shrine-haiden Yabu City Tourism Association] * Access by public transport is difficult (20 minutes by {{ill|Zentan Bus|ja|全但バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zentan Bus|qq=}} from [[:en:Yōka Station|Yōka Station]], followed by a 120-minute walk, approximately 9km). == Surroundings == * {{ill|Saru-otaki Falls|ja|猿尾滝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saru-otaki Falls|qq=}} - Visible from the shrine grounds * {{ill|Yabu Shrine|ja|養父神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yabu Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Manpukuji Temple (Yabu City)|ja|満福寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manpukuji Temple (Yabu City)|qq=}} - Known as "Tajima's Kōyasan" * {{ill|Nikkō-in|ja|日光院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikkō-in|qq=}} * {{ill|Yamada Fūtarō Memorial Museum|ja|山田風太郎記念館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Fūtarō Memorial Museum|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.yabu.hyogo.jp/3445.htm Nagusa Shrine - Yabu City (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://www.city.yabu.hyogo.jp/3466.htm Three-Story Pagoda from Izumo - Yabu City (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://www.city.yabu.hyogo.jp/3467.htm Nagusa Shrine on Myōken Mountain - Yabu City (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp Important Cultural Properties Database: Nagusa Shrine Pagoda (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp Important Cultural Properties Database: Nagusa Shrine Main Hall (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp Important Cultural Properties Database: Nagusa Shrine Worship Hall (Accessed November 1, 2013)] {{Shinto Navbox}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Myōken Faith}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{translated page|ja|名草神社|version=101075941|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[sn:Nagusa Shrine]][Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nagusa Shrine]] [[de:Yatsushiro Shrine]] [[ja:名草神社]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Shinbutsu bunri]] [[Category:Relocated buildings and structures]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Buildings in Yabu, Hyogo]] {{Commons category|Nagusa-jinja}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Nagusa Shrine | location = [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[:en:Yabu, Hyōgo|Yabu City]] Yōka-chō, Ishihara 1755-6 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|25|20.7|N|134|39|20.0|E|scale:20000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Nagusa Shrine}} | deity = Main deity: {{ill|Nagusahiko Ōkami|ja|名草彦大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagusahiko Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
Secondary deities: {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主神|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenominakanushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}}, {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇霊産神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神皇霊産神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Itsu-no-Takeru-no-Kami|ja|五十猛神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsu-no-Takeru-no-Kami|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōyatsuhime|ja|大屋津姫神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyatsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kaya-no-Hime|ja|柧津姫神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaya-no-Hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内小社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}, {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} | founded = | festivals = [[:en:July 18|July 18]] | other_names = Tajima Myōken
Myōken-sha
Taisekiji Myōken-gū
Taisekiji Nikkō-in
Ishiharayama Nikkō-in | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nagusa shrine0590774.JPG|300px]]
Three-storied pagodas | map = {{location map|Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=35|lat_min=25|lat_sec=20.7|lon_deg=134|lon_min=39|lon_sec=20.0|label=Nagusa Shrine|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} [[File:Nagusa-Jinjya Hyōgo001.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Nagusa Shrine''' (名草神社, Nagusa Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Yabu, Hyōgo|Yabu City]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. Its main deity is {{ill|Nagusahiko Ōkami|ja|名草彦大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagusahiko Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The shrine is situated on the slopes of [[:en:Mount Myōken (Tajima)|Myōken Mountain (Yabu City, Kami Town)]] (elevation 1,139m), at approximately 800m above sea level, amidst a forest of giant Myōken cedar trees. Historically, the shrine was known as "Myōken-sha" and served as a major center for Myōken (North Star) worship in the {{ill|San'in Region|ja|山陰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San'in Region|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山陰地方|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山陰地方}} under the patronage of feudal lords such as [[:en:Yamana Sōzen|Yamana Sōzen]] and the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|lt=Tokugawa shogunate|lt=Tokugawa Shogunate|ja|徳川幕府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa Shogunate|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}}. The shrine's main hall, worship hall, and a three-story pagoda, originally from [[:en:Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]], are designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. == History == The shrine is closely associated with {{ill|Nikkō-in|ja|日光院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikkō-in|qq=}}, a temple of the [[:en:Kōyasan Shingon-shū|Kōyasan Shingon School]], located in the Ishihara settlement at the eastern base of the mountain. Before the modern era, the shrine was known as Tajima Myōken and was regarded as one of Japan's three great Myōken sacred sites. The current name "Nagusa Shrine" was adopted following the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism]] during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]]. Ishiharayama Nikkō-in (later known as Taisekiji) is said to have been founded in the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka Period]]. The shrine prospered during the medieval period as a center for Myōken worship under the patronage of the Yamana clan but was devastated by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]’s {{ill|Siege of Chūgoku|ja|中国攻め|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siege of Chūgoku|qq=}} in [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 5 (1577). In [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 9 (1632), the main temple of Ishiharayama was relocated to its current site, which is now Nagusa Shrine. During the Meiji separation of Shinto and Buddhism, Nikkō-in was moved to a lower temple site, while the Myōken Shrine on the mountain became Nagusa Shrine's main hall.『日本歴史地名大系 兵庫県の地名 I』（平凡社、1999）、p.777[http://japan-geographic.tv/hyogo/yabu-nagusajinja.html Nagusa Shrine - Japan Geographic] (Accessed November 2, 2013). == Grounds == === Three-story Pagoda === The pagoda was originally constructed in the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi Period]] during [[:en:Daiei (era)|Daiei]] 7 (1527) in the grounds of [[:en:Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]] by the order of [[:en:Amago Tsunehisa|Amago Tsunehisa]] and was known as the "Kizuki Tower." It was relocated to Nagusa Shrine in [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 5 (1665) as a gift for supplying Myōken cedar timber during Izumo Taisha's reconstruction. * Built in Daiei 7 (1527), relocated in Kanbun 5 (1665), height 23.9m, three-tiered structure with cypress bark roofing * Designated a Specially Protected Building under the {{ill|Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Act|ja|古社寺保存法|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Act|qq=}} in [[:en:1904|Meiji 37]][[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]], now an Important Cultural Property under the {{ill|Cultural Properties Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural Properties Protection Law|qq=}} === Main Hall === * Built in [[:en:Hōreki|Hōreki]] 4 (1754). Measuring nine spans by five spans, single-story, with an irimoya roof and decorative elements * Important Cultural Property (designated in Heisei 22 with attached plaques) === Worship Hall === * Built in [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 2 (1689). Five spans by two spans, single-story, with a cypress bark roof * Important Cultural Property (designated in Heisei 22) == Festivals == * Head Priest Ceremony: [[:en:January 13|January 13]] * Spring Festival: [[:en:May 8|May 8]] * festival: [[:en:July 18|July 18]] * Autumn Festival: [[:en:October 13|October 13]] == Access == * Approximately 30 minutes by car from {{ill|Yōka-Hyōnoyama IC|ja|北近畿豊岡自動車道八鹿氷ノ山IC|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōka-Hyōnoyama IC|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}[https://www.hyogo-tourism.jp/hanadokoro/hanagoyomi.php?num=2&cat=8&area=0 Hyōgo Tourism Guide]. Inaccessible during winter due to snow.[http://www.yabu-kankou.jp/sightseeing/nagusa-shrine-haiden Yabu City Tourism Association] * Access by public transport is difficult (20 minutes by {{ill|Zentan Bus|ja|全但バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zentan Bus|qq=}} from [[:en:Yōka Station|Yōka Station]], followed by a 120-minute walk, approximately 9km). == Surroundings == * {{ill|Saru-otaki Falls|ja|猿尾滝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saru-otaki Falls|qq=}} - Visible from the shrine grounds * {{ill|Yabu Shrine|ja|養父神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yabu Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Manpukuji Temple (Yabu City)|ja|満福寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manpukuji Temple (Yabu City)|qq=}} - Known as "Tajima's Kōyasan" * {{ill|Nikkō-in|ja|日光院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikkō-in|qq=}} * {{ill|Yamada Fūtarō Memorial Museum|ja|山田風太郎記念館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Fūtarō Memorial Museum|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.yabu.hyogo.jp/3445.htm Nagusa Shrine - Yabu City (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://www.city.yabu.hyogo.jp/3466.htm Three-Story Pagoda from Izumo - Yabu City (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://www.city.yabu.hyogo.jp/3467.htm Nagusa Shrine on Myōken Mountain - Yabu City (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp Important Cultural Properties Database: Nagusa Shrine Pagoda (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp Important Cultural Properties Database: Nagusa Shrine Main Hall (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp Important Cultural Properties Database: Nagusa Shrine Worship Hall (Accessed November 1, 2013)] {{Shinto Navbox}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Myōken Faith}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{translated page|ja|名草神社|version=101075941|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[sn:Nagusa Shrine]][Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nagusa Shrine]] [[de:Yatsushiro Shrine]] [[ja:名草神社]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Shinbutsu bunri]] [[Category:Relocated buildings and structures]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Buildings in Yabu, Hyogo]] {{Commons category|Nagusa-jinja}}

Five spans by two spans, single-story, with a cypress bark roof * Important Cultural Property (designated in Heisei 22) == Festivals == * Head Priest Ceremony: [[:en:January 13|January 13]] * Spring Festival: [[:en:May 8|May 8]] * festival: [[:en:July 18|July 18]] * Autumn Festival: [[:en:October 13|October 13]] == Access == * Approximately 30 minutes by car from {{ill|Yōka-Hyōnoyama IC|ja|北近畿豊岡自動車道八鹿氷ノ山IC|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōka-Hyōnoyama IC|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}[https://www.hyogo-tourism.jp/hanadokoro/hanagoyomi.php?num=2&cat=8&area=0 Hyōgo Tourism Guide]. Inaccessible during winter due to snow.[http://www.yabu-kankou.jp/sightseeing/nagusa-shrine-haiden Yabu City Tourism Association] * Access by public transport is difficult (20 minutes by {{ill|Zentan Bus|ja|全但バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zentan Bus|qq=}} from [[:en:Yōka Station|Yōka Station]], followed by a 120-minute walk, approximately 9km). == Surroundings == * {{ill|Saru-otaki Falls|ja|猿尾滝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saru-otaki Falls|qq=}} - Visible from the shrine grounds * {{ill|Yabu Shrine|ja|養父神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yabu Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Manpukuji Temple (Yabu City)|ja|満福寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manpukuji Temple (Yabu City)|qq=}} - Known as "Tajima's Kōyasan" * {{ill|Nikkō-in|ja|日光院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikkō-in|qq=}} * {{ill|Yamada Fūtarō Memorial Museum|ja|山田風太郎記念館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Fūtarō Memorial Museum|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.yabu.hyogo.jp/3445.htm Nagusa Shrine - Yabu City (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp Important Cultural Properties Database: Nagusa Shrine Pagoda (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp Important Cultural Properties Database: Nagusa Shrine Main Hall (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp Important Cultural Properties Database: Nagusa Shrine Worship Hall (Accessed November 1, 2013)] {{Shinto Navbox}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Myōken Faith}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{translated page|ja|名草神社|version=101075941|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[sn:Nagusa Shrine]][Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nagusa Shrine]] [[de:Yatsushiro Shrine]] [[ja:名草神社]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Shinbutsu bunri]] [[Category:Relocated buildings and structures]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Buildings in Yabu, Hyogo]] {{Commons category|Nagusa-jinja}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Nagusa Shrine | location = [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[:en:Yabu, Hyōgo|Yabu City]] Yōka-chō, Ishihara 1755-6 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|25|20.7|N|134|39|20.0|E|scale:20000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Nagusa Shrine}} | deity = Main deity: {{ill|Nagusahiko Ōkami|ja|名草彦大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagusahiko Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
Secondary deities: {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主神|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenominakanushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}}, {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇霊産神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神皇霊産神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Itsu-no-Takeru-no-Kami|ja|五十猛神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsu-no-Takeru-no-Kami|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōyatsuhime|ja|大屋津姫神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyatsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kaya-no-Hime|ja|柧津姫神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaya-no-Hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内小社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}, {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} | founded = | festivals = [[:en:July 18|July 18]] | other_names = Tajima Myōken
Myōken-sha
Taisekiji Myōken-gū
Taisekiji Nikkō-in
Ishiharayama Nikkō-in | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nagusa shrine0590774.JPG|300px]]
Three-storied pagodas | map = {{location map|Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=35|lat_min=25|lat_sec=20.7|lon_deg=134|lon_min=39|lon_sec=20.0|label=Nagusa Shrine|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} [[File:Nagusa-Jinjya Hyōgo001.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Nagusa Shrine''' (名草神社, Nagusa Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Yabu, Hyōgo|Yabu City]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. Its main deity is {{ill|Nagusahiko Ōkami|ja|名草彦大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagusahiko Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The shrine is situated on the slopes of [[:en:Mount Myōken (Tajima)|Myōken Mountain (Yabu City, Kami Town)]] (elevation 1,139m), at approximately 800m above sea level, amidst a forest of giant Myōken cedar trees. Historically, the shrine was known as "Myōken-sha" and served as a major center for Myōken (North Star) worship in the {{ill|San'in Region|ja|山陰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San'in Region|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山陰地方|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山陰地方}} under the patronage of feudal lords such as [[:en:Yamana Sōzen|Yamana Sōzen]] and the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|lt=Tokugawa shogunate|lt=Tokugawa Shogunate|ja|徳川幕府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa Shogunate|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}}. The shrine's main hall, worship hall, and a three-story pagoda, originally from [[:en:Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]], are designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. == History == The shrine is closely associated with {{ill|Nikkō-in|ja|日光院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikkō-in|qq=}}, a temple of the [[:en:Kōyasan Shingon-shū|Kōyasan Shingon School]], located in the Ishihara settlement at the eastern base of the mountain. Before the modern era, the shrine was known as Tajima Myōken and was regarded as one of Japan's three great Myōken sacred sites. The current name "Nagusa Shrine" was adopted following the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism]] during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]]. Ishiharayama Nikkō-in (later known as Taisekiji) is said to have been founded in the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka Period]]. The shrine prospered during the medieval period as a center for Myōken worship under the patronage of the Yamana clan but was devastated by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]’s {{ill|Siege of Chūgoku|ja|中国攻め|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siege of Chūgoku|qq=}} in [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 5 (1577). In [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 9 (1632), the main temple of Ishiharayama was relocated to its current site, which is now Nagusa Shrine. During the Meiji separation of Shinto and Buddhism, Nikkō-in was moved to a lower temple site, while the Myōken Shrine on the mountain became Nagusa Shrine's main hall.『日本歴史地名大系 兵庫県の地名 I』（平凡社、1999）、p.777[http://japan-geographic.tv/hyogo/yabu-nagusajinja.html Nagusa Shrine - Japan Geographic] (Accessed November 2, 2013). == Grounds == === Three-story Pagoda === The pagoda was originally constructed in the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi Period]] during [[:en:Daiei (era)|Daiei]] 7 (1527) in the grounds of [[:en:Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]] by the order of [[:en:Amago Tsunehisa|Amago Tsunehisa]] and was known as the "Kizuki Tower." It was relocated to Nagusa Shrine in [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 5 (1665) as a gift for supplying Myōken cedar timber during Izumo Taisha's reconstruction. * Built in Daiei 7 (1527), relocated in Kanbun 5 (1665), height 23.9m, three-tiered structure with cypress bark roofing * Designated a Specially Protected Building under the {{ill|Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Act|ja|古社寺保存法|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Act|qq=}} in [[:en:1904|Meiji 37]], now an Important Cultural Property under the {{ill|Cultural Properties Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural Properties Protection Law|qq=}} === Main Hall === * Built in [[:en:Hōreki|Hōreki]] 4 (1754). Measuring nine spans by five spans, single-story, with an irimoya roof and decorative elements * Important Cultural Property (designated in Heisei 22 with attached plaques) === Worship Hall === * Built in [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 2 (1689). Five spans by two spans, single-story, with a cypress bark roof * Important Cultural Property (designated in Heisei 22) == Festivals == * Head Priest Ceremony: [[:en:January 13|January 13]] * Spring Festival: [[:en:May 8|May 8]] * festival: [[:en:July 18|July 18]] * Autumn Festival: [[:en:October 13|October 13]] == Access == * Approximately 30 minutes by car from {{ill|Yōka-Hyōnoyama IC|ja|北近畿豊岡自動車道八鹿氷ノ山IC|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōka-Hyōnoyama IC|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}[https://www.hyogo-tourism.jp/hanadokoro/hanagoyomi.php?num=2&cat=8&area=0 Hyōgo Tourism Guide]. Inaccessible during winter due to snow.[http://www.yabu-kankou.jp/sightseeing/nagusa-shrine-haiden Yabu City Tourism Association] * Access by public transport is difficult (20 minutes by {{ill|Zentan Bus|ja|全但バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zentan Bus|qq=}} from [[:en:Yōka Station|Yōka Station]], followed by a 120-minute walk, approximately 9km). == Surroundings == * {{ill|Saru-otaki Falls|ja|猿尾滝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saru-otaki Falls|qq=}} - Visible from the shrine grounds * {{ill|Yabu Shrine|ja|養父神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yabu Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Manpukuji Temple (Yabu City)|ja|満福寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manpukuji Temple (Yabu City)|qq=}} - Known as "Tajima's Kōyasan" * {{ill|Nikkō-in|ja|日光院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikkō-in|qq=}} * {{ill|Yamada Fūtarō Memorial Museum|ja|山田風太郎記念館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Fūtarō Memorial Museum|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.yabu.hyogo.jp/3445.htm Nagusa Shrine - Yabu City (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://www.city.yabu.hyogo.jp/3466.htm Three-Story Pagoda from Izumo - Yabu City (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://www.city.yabu.hyogo.jp/3467.htm Nagusa Shrine on Myōken Mountain - Yabu City (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp Important Cultural Properties Database: Nagusa Shrine Pagoda (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp Important Cultural Properties Database: Nagusa Shrine Main Hall (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp Important Cultural Properties Database: Nagusa Shrine Worship Hall (Accessed November 1, 2013)] {{Shinto Navbox}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Myōken Faith}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{translated page|ja|名草神社|version=101075941|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[sn:Nagusa Shrine]][Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nagusa Shrine]] [[de:Yatsushiro Shrine]] [[ja:名草神社]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Shinbutsu bunri]] [[Category:Relocated buildings and structures]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Buildings in Yabu, Hyogo]] {{Commons category|Nagusa-jinja}}

Known as "Tajima's Kōyasan" * {{ill|Nikkō-in|ja|日光院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikkō-in|qq=}} * {{ill|Yamada Fūtarō Memorial Museum|ja|山田風太郎記念館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Fūtarō Memorial Museum|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.yabu.hyogo.jp/3445.htm Nagusa Shrine - Yabu City (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://www.city.yabu.hyogo.jp/3466.htm Three-Story Pagoda from Izumo - Yabu City (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://www.city.yabu.hyogo.jp/3467.htm Nagusa Shrine on Myōken Mountain - Yabu City (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp Important Cultural Properties Database: Nagusa Shrine Pagoda (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp Important Cultural Properties Database: Nagusa Shrine Main Hall (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp Important Cultural Properties Database: Nagusa Shrine Worship Hall (Accessed November 1, 2013)] {{Shinto Navbox}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Myōken Faith}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{translated page|ja|名草神社|version=101075941|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[sn:Nagusa Shrine]][Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nagusa Shrine]] [[de:Yatsushiro Shrine]] [[ja:名草神社]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Shinbutsu bunri]] [[Category:Relocated buildings and structures]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Buildings in Yabu, Hyogo]] {{Commons category|Nagusa-jinja}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Nagusa Shrine | location = [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[:en:Yabu, Hyōgo|Yabu City]] Yōka-chō, Ishihara 1755-6 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|25|20.7|N|134|39|20.0|E|scale:20000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Nagusa Shrine}} | deity = Main deity: {{ill|Nagusahiko Ōkami|ja|名草彦大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagusahiko Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
Secondary deities: {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主神|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenominakanushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}}, {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇霊産神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神皇霊産神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Itsu-no-Takeru-no-Kami|ja|五十猛神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsu-no-Takeru-no-Kami|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōyatsuhime|ja|大屋津姫神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyatsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kaya-no-Hime|ja|柧津姫神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaya-no-Hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内小社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}}, {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} | founded = | festivals = [[:en:July 18|July 18]] | other_names = Tajima Myōken
Myōken-sha
Taisekiji Myōken-gū
Taisekiji Nikkō-in
Ishiharayama Nikkō-in | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nagusa shrine0590774.JPG|300px]]
Three-storied pagodas | map = {{location map|Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=35|lat_min=25|lat_sec=20.7|lon_deg=134|lon_min=39|lon_sec=20.0|label=Nagusa Shrine|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} [[File:Nagusa-Jinjya Hyōgo001.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Nagusa Shrine''' (名草神社, Nagusa Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Yabu, Hyōgo|Yabu City]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. Its main deity is {{ill|Nagusahiko Ōkami|ja|名草彦大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagusahiko Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The shrine is situated on the slopes of [[:en:Mount Myōken (Tajima)|Myōken Mountain (Yabu City, Kami Town)]] (elevation 1,139m), at approximately 800m above sea level, amidst a forest of giant Myōken cedar trees. Historically, the shrine was known as "Myōken-sha" and served as a major center for Myōken (North Star) worship in the {{ill|San'in Region|ja|山陰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San'in Region|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山陰地方|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山陰地方}} under the patronage of feudal lords such as [[:en:Yamana Sōzen|Yamana Sōzen]] and the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|lt=Tokugawa shogunate|lt=Tokugawa Shogunate|ja|徳川幕府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa Shogunate|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}}. The shrine's main hall, worship hall, and a three-story pagoda, originally from [[:en:Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]], are designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. == History == The shrine is closely associated with {{ill|Nikkō-in|ja|日光院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikkō-in|qq=}}, a temple of the [[:en:Kōyasan Shingon-shū|Kōyasan Shingon School]], located in the Ishihara settlement at the eastern base of the mountain. Before the modern era, the shrine was known as Tajima Myōken and was regarded as one of Japan's three great Myōken sacred sites. The current name "Nagusa Shrine" was adopted following the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism]] during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]]. Ishiharayama Nikkō-in (later known as Taisekiji) is said to have been founded in the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka Period]]. The shrine prospered during the medieval period as a center for Myōken worship under the patronage of the Yamana clan but was devastated by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]’s {{ill|Siege of Chūgoku|ja|中国攻め|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siege of Chūgoku|qq=}} in [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 5 (1577). In [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 9 (1632), the main temple of Ishiharayama was relocated to its current site, which is now Nagusa Shrine. During the Meiji separation of Shinto and Buddhism, Nikkō-in was moved to a lower temple site, while the Myōken Shrine on the mountain became Nagusa Shrine's main hall.『日本歴史地名大系 兵庫県の地名 I』（平凡社、1999）、p.777[http://japan-geographic.tv/hyogo/yabu-nagusajinja.html Nagusa Shrine - Japan Geographic] (Accessed November 2, 2013). == Grounds == === Three-story Pagoda === The pagoda was originally constructed in the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi Period]] during [[:en:Daiei (era)|Daiei]] 7 (1527) in the grounds of [[:en:Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]] by the order of [[:en:Amago Tsunehisa|Amago Tsunehisa]] and was known as the "Kizuki Tower." It was relocated to Nagusa Shrine in [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 5 (1665) as a gift for supplying Myōken cedar timber during Izumo Taisha's reconstruction. * Built in Daiei 7 (1527), relocated in Kanbun 5 (1665), height 23.9m, three-tiered structure with cypress bark roofing * Designated a Specially Protected Building under the {{ill|Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Act|ja|古社寺保存法|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Act|qq=}} in [[:en:1904|Meiji 37]], now an Important Cultural Property under the {{ill|Cultural Properties Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural Properties Protection Law|qq=}} === Main Hall === * Built in [[:en:Hōreki|Hōreki]] 4 (1754). Measuring nine spans by five spans, single-story, with an irimoya roof and decorative elements * Important Cultural Property (designated in Heisei 22 with attached plaques) === Worship Hall === * Built in [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 2 (1689). Five spans by two spans, single-story, with a cypress bark roof * Important Cultural Property (designated in Heisei 22) == Festivals == * Head Priest Ceremony: [[:en:January 13|January 13]] * Spring Festival: [[:en:May 8|May 8]] * festival: [[:en:July 18|July 18]] * Autumn Festival: [[:en:October 13|October 13]] == Access == * Approximately 30 minutes by car from {{ill|Yōka-Hyōnoyama IC|ja|北近畿豊岡自動車道八鹿氷ノ山IC|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōka-Hyōnoyama IC|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}[https://www.hyogo-tourism.jp/hanadokoro/hanagoyomi.php?num=2&cat=8&area=0 Hyōgo Tourism Guide]. Inaccessible during winter due to snow.[http://www.yabu-kankou.jp/sightseeing/nagusa-shrine-haiden Yabu City Tourism Association] * Access by public transport is difficult (20 minutes by {{ill|Zentan Bus|ja|全但バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zentan Bus|qq=}} from [[:en:Yōka Station|Yōka Station]], followed by a 120-minute walk, approximately 9km). == Surroundings == * {{ill|Saru-otaki Falls|ja|猿尾滝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saru-otaki Falls|qq=}} - Visible from the shrine grounds * {{ill|Yabu Shrine|ja|養父神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yabu Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Manpukuji Temple (Yabu City)|ja|満福寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manpukuji Temple (Yabu City)|qq=}} - Known as "Tajima's Kōyasan" * {{ill|Nikkō-in|ja|日光院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikkō-in|qq=}} * {{ill|Yamada Fūtarō Memorial Museum|ja|山田風太郎記念館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Fūtarō Memorial Museum|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.yabu.hyogo.jp/3445.htm Nagusa Shrine - Yabu City (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://www.city.yabu.hyogo.jp/3466.htm Three-Story Pagoda from Izumo - Yabu City (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://www.city.yabu.hyogo.jp/3467.htm Nagusa Shrine on Myōken Mountain - Yabu City (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp Important Cultural Properties Database: Nagusa Shrine Pagoda (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp Important Cultural Properties Database: Nagusa Shrine Main Hall (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp Important Cultural Properties Database: Nagusa Shrine Worship Hall (Accessed November 1, 2013)] {{Shinto Navbox}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Myōken Faith}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{translated page|ja|名草神社|version=101075941|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[sn:Nagusa Shrine]][Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nagusa Shrine]] [[de:Yatsushiro Shrine]] [[ja:名草神社]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Shinbutsu bunri]] [[Category:Relocated buildings and structures]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Buildings in Yabu, Hyogo]] {{Commons category|Nagusa-jinja}}

Yabu City (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp Important Cultural Properties Database: Nagusa Shrine Pagoda (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp Important Cultural Properties Database: Nagusa Shrine Main Hall (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp Important Cultural Properties Database: Nagusa Shrine Worship Hall (Accessed November 1, 2013)] {{Shinto Navbox}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Myōken Faith}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{translated page|ja|名草神社|version=101075941|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[sn:Nagusa Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nagusa Shrine]] [[de:Yatsushiro Shrine]] [[ja:名草神社]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Shinbutsu bunri]] [[Category:Relocated buildings and structures]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Buildings in Yabu, Hyogo]] {{Commons category|Nagusa-jinja}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17229499]] Wikidata name: Nagusa-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Nagusa Shrine | location = [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[:en:Yabu, Hyōgo|Yabu City]] Yōka-chō, Ishihara 1755-6 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|25|20.7|N|134|39|20.0|E|scale:20000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Nagusa Shrine}} | deity = Main deity: {{ill|Nagusahiko Ōkami|ja|名草彦大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagusahiko Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
Secondary deities: {{ill|Amenominakanushi|ja|天御中主神|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenominakanushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}}, {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇霊産神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神皇霊産神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Itsu-no-Takeru-no-Kami|ja|五十猛神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsu-no-Takeru-no-Kami|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōyatsuhime|ja|大屋津姫神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyatsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kaya-no-Hime|ja|柧津姫神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaya-no-Hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内小社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}}, {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} | founded = | festivals = [[:en:July 18|July 18]] | other_names = Tajima Myōken
Myōken-sha
Taisekiji Myōken-gū
Taisekiji Nikkō-in
Ishiharayama Nikkō-in | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nagusa shrine0590774.JPG|300px]]
Three-storied pagodas | map = {{location map|Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=35|lat_min=25|lat_sec=20.7|lon_deg=134|lon_min=39|lon_sec=20.0|label=Nagusa Shrine|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} [[File:Nagusa-Jinjya Hyōgo001.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Nagusa Shrine''' (名草神社, Nagusa Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Yabu, Hyōgo|Yabu City]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. Its main deity is {{ill|Nagusahiko Ōkami|ja|名草彦大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagusahiko Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The shrine is situated on the slopes of [[:en:Mount Myōken (Tajima)|Myōken Mountain (Yabu City, Kami Town)]] (elevation 1,139m), at approximately 800m above sea level, amidst a forest of giant Myōken cedar trees. Historically, the shrine was known as "Myōken-sha" and served as a major center for Myōken (North Star) worship in the {{ill|San'in Region|ja|山陰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San'in Region|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山陰地方|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山陰地方}} under the patronage of feudal lords such as [[:en:Yamana Sōzen|Yamana Sōzen]] and the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|lt=Tokugawa shogunate|lt=Tokugawa Shogunate|ja|徳川幕府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa Shogunate|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}}. The shrine's main hall, worship hall, and a three-story pagoda, originally from [[:en:Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]], are designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. == History == The shrine is closely associated with {{ill|Nikkō-in|ja|日光院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikkō-in|qq=}}, a temple of the [[:en:Kōyasan Shingon-shū|Kōyasan Shingon School]], located in the Ishihara settlement at the eastern base of the mountain. Before the modern era, the shrine was known as Tajima Myōken and was regarded as one of Japan's three great Myōken sacred sites. The current name "Nagusa Shrine" was adopted following the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism]] during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]]. Ishiharayama Nikkō-in (later known as Taisekiji) is said to have been founded in the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka Period]]. The shrine prospered during the medieval period as a center for Myōken worship under the patronage of the Yamana clan but was devastated by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]’s {{ill|Siege of Chūgoku|ja|中国攻め|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siege of Chūgoku|qq=}} in [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 5 (1577). In [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 9 (1632), the main temple of Ishiharayama was relocated to its current site, which is now Nagusa Shrine. During the Meiji separation of Shinto and Buddhism, Nikkō-in was moved to a lower temple site, while the Myōken Shrine on the mountain became Nagusa Shrine's main hall.『日本歴史地名大系 兵庫県の地名 I』（平凡社、1999）、p.777[http://japan-geographic.tv/hyogo/yabu-nagusajinja.html Nagusa Shrine - Japan Geographic] (Accessed November 2, 2013). == Grounds == === Three-story Pagoda === The pagoda was originally constructed in the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi Period]] during [[:en:Daiei (era)|Daiei]] 7 (1527) in the grounds of [[:en:Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]] by the order of [[:en:Amago Tsunehisa|Amago Tsunehisa]] and was known as the "Kizuki Tower." It was relocated to Nagusa Shrine in [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 5 (1665) as a gift for supplying Myōken cedar timber during Izumo Taisha's reconstruction. * Built in Daiei 7 (1527), relocated in Kanbun 5 (1665), height 23.9m, three-tiered structure with cypress bark roofing * Designated a Specially Protected Building under the {{ill|Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Act|ja|古社寺保存法|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Act|qq=}} in [[:en:1904|Meiji 37]], now an Important Cultural Property under the {{ill|Cultural Properties Protection Law|ja|文化財保護法|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural Properties Protection Law|qq=}} === Main Hall === * Built in [[:en:Hōreki|Hōreki]] 4 (1754). Measuring nine spans by five spans, single-story, with an irimoya roof and decorative elements * Important Cultural Property (designated in Heisei 22 with attached plaques) === Worship Hall === * Built in [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 2 (1689). Five spans by two spans, single-story, with a cypress bark roof * Important Cultural Property (designated in Heisei 22) == Festivals == * Head Priest Ceremony: [[:en:January 13|January 13]] * Spring Festival: [[:en:May 8|May 8]] * festival: [[:en:July 18|July 18]] * Autumn Festival: [[:en:October 13|October 13]] == Access == * Approximately 30 minutes by car from {{ill|Yōka-Hyōnoyama IC|ja|北近畿豊岡自動車道八鹿氷ノ山IC|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōka-Hyōnoyama IC|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}[https://www.hyogo-tourism.jp/hanadokoro/hanagoyomi.php?num=2&cat=8&area=0 Hyōgo Tourism Guide]. Inaccessible during winter due to snow.[http://www.yabu-kankou.jp/sightseeing/nagusa-shrine-haiden Yabu City Tourism Association] * Access by public transport is difficult (20 minutes by {{ill|Zentan Bus|ja|全但バス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zentan Bus|qq=}} from [[:en:Yōka Station|Yōka Station]], followed by a 120-minute walk, approximately 9km). == Surroundings == * {{ill|Saru-otaki Falls|ja|猿尾滝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saru-otaki Falls|qq=}} - Visible from the shrine grounds * {{ill|Yabu Shrine|ja|養父神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yabu Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Manpukuji Temple (Yabu City)|ja|満福寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manpukuji Temple (Yabu City)|qq=}} - Known as "Tajima's Kōyasan" * {{ill|Nikkō-in|ja|日光院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikkō-in|qq=}} * {{ill|Yamada Fūtarō Memorial Museum|ja|山田風太郎記念館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Fūtarō Memorial Museum|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.yabu.hyogo.jp/3445.htm Nagusa Shrine - Yabu City (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://www.city.yabu.hyogo.jp/3466.htm Three-Story Pagoda from Izumo - Yabu City (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://www.city.yabu.hyogo.jp/3467.htm Nagusa Shrine on Myōken Mountain - Yabu City (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp Important Cultural Properties Database: Nagusa Shrine Pagoda (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp Important Cultural Properties Database: Nagusa Shrine Main Hall (Accessed November 1, 2013)] * [http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp Important Cultural Properties Database: Nagusa Shrine Worship Hall (Accessed November 1, 2013)] {{Shinto Navbox}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Myōken Faith}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{translated page|ja|名草神社|version=101075941|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tajima Province]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[sn:Nagusa Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nagusa Shrine]] [[de:Yatsushiro Shrine]] [[ja:名草神社]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Shinbutsu bunri]] [[Category:Relocated buildings and structures]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Buildings in Yabu, Hyogo]] {{Commons category|Nagusa-jinja}}
